  start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the attorney\'s appearance began. Supports accurate time tracking for billing and utilization reporting.']
  transcript_ordered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a transcript of the proceeding has been ordered. True if transcript ordered, False otherwise. Used for document management and billing tracking.']
  travel_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Time spent traveling to and from the appearance location, measured in hours. May be billable or non-billable depending on client agreement and Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) terms.']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'The UTBMS activity code providing granular detail on the specific activity performed during the appearance. Used for detailed billing analysis and client reporting.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'The UTBMS task code associated with this appearance for standardized billing and matter management. Enables consistent categorization of legal work across firms and clients.']

  Note: 'Records each attorney appearance at a court proceeding, arbitration session, or ADR hearing. Stores timekeeper reference, docket reference, hearing reference, appearance type (lead counsel, co-counsel, local counsel, observer), pro hac vice status, bar admission jurisdiction, appearance date, outcome notes, and time spent. Supports billing verification, court-mandated appearance reporting, and attorney utilization analytics.'
}

  filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the filing was submitted to the court system, critical for deadline compliance and time-stamped filings.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'The category or type of document being filed with the court. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: complaint|motion|brief|answer|notice|order|judgment|petition|affidavit|exhibit — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  page_count varchar [note: 'The total number of pages in the filed document.']

  sealed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing has been sealed by court order and is not available for public access.']
  service_date date [note: 'The date on which the filing was served on the opposing parties or other required recipients.']
  service_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the filing was served on opposing parties or the court.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every document filed with a court, tribunal, or arbitral body. Captures filing type (complaint, motion, brief, answer, notice, order, judgment), filing date/time, ECF confirmation number, PACER transaction ID, filed-by attorney, filing fee amount, service method, acceptance status (accepted, rejected, pending), and deadline compliance flag. Links to document management for source document reference and supports cost recovery allocation for client billing.'
}

Table matter.deadline {
  deadline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for deadline']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Critical deadlines often require specific client contact action (document production, settlement authority, filing approvals). This link enables targeted deadline notifications, tracks client responsi']
  court_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.court_rule. Business justification: Matter deadlines are frequently governed by specific procedural court rules (e.g., FRCP Rule 26 disclosure deadlines, local rules for motion practice). The matter_deadline table currently stores court']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the docket or case to which this deadline applies.']
  hearing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.hearing. Business justification: Court deadlines are often set during hearings or are hearing-related (e.g., deadline to file pre-hearing briefs). The matter_deadline table does not currently link to the hearing entity. This FK allow']
  judge_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the judge who issued the order or ruling establishing this deadline.']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Deadlines are triggered by or responsive to specific documents (e.g., complaint triggers answer deadline). matter_deadline.document_reference is a plain-text denormalization. A proper FK enables autom']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal matter associated with this deadline.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.obligation. Business justification: Contractual obligation due dates are formally docketed as matter deadlines for malpractice risk management. matter_deadline has regulatory_obligation_id (compliance domain) but no FK to contract.oblig']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.order. Business justification: Court orders frequently impose deadlines (e.g., deadline to respond to a motion, deadline to produce documents). The matter_deadline table has court_rule_citation and triggering_event fields but no di']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_profile. Business justification: Court deadlines are client-specific obligations. Direct link enables client-facing deadline dashboards, malpractice risk monitoring per client, client notification workflows, and compliance reporting ']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Missed court deadlines are the leading cause of legal malpractice claims. Risk management systems track deadline compliance failures for insurer reporting and practice quality control.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Legal deadlines are often imposed by regulatory obligations (filing deadlines, response deadlines, compliance certification deadlines, regulatory notice requirements). Tracking which regulatory obliga']
  tribunal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the court or tribunal that imposed or governs this deadline.']
  adr_proceeding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deadline is associated with an ADR proceeding such as arbitration or mediation (True/False).']
  adr_proceeding_type varchar [note: 'Type of ADR proceeding if applicable (e.g., arbitration, mediation, settlement conference, early neutral evaluation).']
  alert_days_before varchar [note: 'Number of days before the due date when alerts or reminders should be triggered.']
  alert_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an alert or reminder has been sent for this deadline (True/False).']
  alert_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the most recent alert was sent. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  calendar_entry_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the calendar or docket control system entry associated with this deadline for tracking and reminder purposes.']
  calendar_sync_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether this deadline has been successfully synchronized with the firm-wide court calendar system.']
  calendar_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the deadline was last synchronized with the calendar system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  client_notified_date date [note: 'The date on which the client was notified of this deadline, particularly important for critical deadlines requiring client input or approval.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the required action was completed and the deadline was satisfied. Null if not yet completed.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting how the deadline was satisfied, any issues encountered, or reasons for missed or waived status.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the deadline: pending (not yet due), completed (satisfied on time), missed (deadline passed without completion), waived (deadline no longer applicable), extended (deadline date moved).']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the deadline was marked as completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  court_case_number varchar [note: 'The official case number or docket number assigned by the court or tribunal, if this deadline is associated with litigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this deadline record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deadline_date date [note: 'The calendar date by which the required action must be completed. This is the hard deadline for compliance or filing.']
  deadline_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the deadline, including the specific action required, the legal basis, and any relevant context or instructions.']
  deadline_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp (date and time) by which the action must be completed, used when time-of-day precision is required (e.g., court filing cutoff at 11:59 PM).']
  deadline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deadline by its legal or procedural nature (e.g., statute of limitations, court-ordered deadline, regulatory filing deadline, contractual notice period, discovery cutoff, motion filing deadline).']
  due_date date [note: 'The calculated date by which the deadline must be met. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  due_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp by which the deadline must be met, including time of day. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  ecf_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deadline requires an electronic filing through the courts ECF system (True/False).']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deadline requires escalation to senior management or the supervising partner if not completed within the reminder window.']
  extended_deadline_date date [note: 'The new deadline date after an extension has been granted. Null if no extension has been granted.']
  extended_due_date date [note: 'The new due date after an extension has been granted. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  extension_granted_date date [note: 'The date on which an extension to the original deadline was granted by the court, opposing counsel, or regulatory body.']
  extension_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an extension of time was granted for this deadline (True/False).']
  extension_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or citation to the court order granting the extension of time.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the deadline action must be filed or delivered (electronic filing, paper filing, hand delivery, certified mail).']
  governing_rule_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the statute, rule, regulation, or contractual provision that establishes this deadline (e.g., FRCP Rule 56(c), 28 U.S.C. § 1658, Contract Section 12.3).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deadline is currently active and requires monitoring. Inactive deadlines are historical or cancelled.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction or court system under which this deadline is governed (e.g., Federal District Court Southern District of New York, California Superior Court, SEC).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this deadline record was last updated in the system.']
  malpractice_insurance_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the firms malpractice insurance carrier has been notified of a missed deadline or potential claim related to this deadline.']
  malpractice_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether missing this deadline poses a significant malpractice risk (True/False). Used for risk management and quality control.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this deadline record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this deadline record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the deadline, including special instructions, dependencies, or context.']
  opposing_counsel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the opposing counsel or law firm involved in the matter, relevant for deadlines involving adversarial proceedings.']
  pacer_case_number varchar [note: 'The PACER case number associated with this deadline for federal court cases.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification of the deadline based on risk, client impact, and legal consequences (critical, high, medium, low).']
  reminder_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the deadline date when the first reminder notification should be triggered to the responsible timekeeper.']
  responsible_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the practice group or team responsible for this deadline.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the malpractice or business risk associated with missing this deadline (high, medium, low).']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'The event or action that initiated the deadline calculation (e.g., service of complaint, court order issued, notice of appeal filed).']
  triggering_event_date date [note: 'The date on which the triggering event occurred. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this deadline record.']

  Note: 'Tracks all court-imposed and rule-based deadlines associated with a docket or ADR proceeding. Stores deadline type (answer due, discovery cutoff, motion deadline, trial date, appeal window, briefing deadline), calculated due date, triggering event, applicable court rule citation, responsible attorney, completion status, extension granted flag, extension order reference, and calendar synchronization status. Critical for docket management, malpractice risk avoidance, and firm-wide court calendar views.'
}

Table matter.order {
  order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order']
  court_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the court or tribunal that issued the order.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the docket or case to which this order pertains.']
  hearing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.hearing. Business justification: Court orders are frequently issued during or immediately following hearings. The order table has hearing_date as a STRING but no FK to the hearing entity. Adding this FK allows direct linkage to the h']
  indemnity_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.indemnity_claim. Business justification: Court orders in indemnity litigation (discovery orders, summary judgment) affect claim management, defense strategy, and insurer coordination; essential for claims workflow.']
  issuing_judge_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the judge who issued or signed the order.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal matter associated with this court order.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_profile. Business justification: Court orders directly affect clients. Linking supports client outcome tracking, win/loss reporting, appeals deadline monitoring, client communication about order compliance, and litigation portfolio a']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Adverse court orders (sanctions, fee awards, injunctions against clients) generate risk events requiring documentation for practice management, client communication, and potential malpractice defense.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: A court order (consent decree, injunction, compliance order) may impose or directly reference a regulatory obligation. Linking order to regulatory_obligation enables compliance teams to track court-im']
  adr_proceeding_type varchar [note: 'If the order relates to or arises from an Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) proceeding, specifies the type: mediation, arbitration (including ICC, LCIA), settlement conference, or none if not ADR-related.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'The last date on which a party may file an appeal or motion to reconsider this order, as determined by applicable court rules.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'The date by which parties must comply with the directives or obligations set forth in the order. Null if no specific deadline is imposed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for any monetary amounts in the order (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Null if no monetary amounts are involved.']
  document_storage_path varchar [note: 'Internal file path or document management system (DMS) reference where the order document is stored (e.g., iManage Work, NetDocuments).']
  ecf_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number assigned by the court\'s Electronic Case Filing (ECF) system. Used for PACER retrieval and docket tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the order becomes legally effective and enforceable. May differ from issue date if the order specifies a future effective date.']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of enforcement or compliance activities: not_required (order is declaratory or self-executing), pending (enforcement not yet initiated), in_progress (active enforcement), completed (obligations fulfilled), or contested (enforcement disputed).']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the order was filed or entered: ECF (Electronic Case Filing system), paper (physical filing), email, or fax.']
  is_appealable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is immediately appealable (true) or interlocutory and not subject to immediate appeal (false).']
  is_final boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is a final order disposing of all claims and parties (true) or an interlocutory order (false).']
  is_published boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is published in official court reporters or legal databases (true) or remains unpublished (false). Published orders may have precedential value.']
  is_sealed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is under seal (true) and subject to restricted access, or publicly accessible (false).']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the court issued or entered the order. This is the principal business event timestamp for the order.']
  monetary_award_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount awarded or ordered to be paid, if the order includes a financial judgment or damages award. Null if the order does not involve monetary relief.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes, annotations, or strategic commentary regarding the order, its implications, or required follow-up actions. Confidential attorney work product.']
  order_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the order\'s subject matter: discovery (document production, depositions), motion (ruling on a motion), scheduling (case management), judgment (final disposition), sentencing (criminal penalty), or relief (equitable remedy).']
  order_number varchar [note: 'The court-assigned or internally-assigned unique number identifying this order. This is the externally-known business identifier.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the order: draft (prepared but not issued), issued (entered by court), stayed (temporarily suspended), vacated (nullified), appealed (under appellate review), enforced (execution in progress), or complied (obligations fulfilled). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|stayed|vacated|appealed|enforced|complied — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_text varchar [note: 'The complete text of the court order as issued. May contain confidential case details and party information.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order by its legal nature: procedural (scheduling, discovery), substantive (merits decision), consent (agreed by parties), default (non-appearance), summary judgment (pre-trial disposition), or injunction (equitable relief).']
  pacer_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL to the order document in the PACER (Public Access to Court Electronic Records) system for federal court orders.']
  prevailing_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party or side prevailed or was granted relief by the order. \'none\' indicates the order was neutral or procedural without a clear prevailing party. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: plaintiff|defendant|petitioner|respondent|movant|non_movant|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  summary varchar [note: 'A concise textual summary of the order\'s content, directives, and key holdings. Provides quick reference without reading the full order text.']
  title varchar [note: 'The formal title or caption of the order as it appears in court records (e.g., \'Order Granting Motion for Summary Judgment\', \'Temporary Restraining Order\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order record was last modified. Audit trail field.']

  Note: 'Records judicial orders, rulings, and decisions issued in connection with a docket. Captures order type (procedural, substantive, consent, default, summary judgment, injunction), issue date, issuing judge, order text summary, compliance deadline, appeal deadline, enforcement status, and whether the order is published or unpublished. Distinct from filed documents — this is the authoritative record of what the court has directed.'
}

Table matter.court_rule {
  court_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the court rule record. Primary key.']
  doc_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.doc_template. Business justification: Court rules mandate specific document templates and mandatory forms. court_rule.mandatory_form_reference is a plain-text denormalization. A proper FK enables automated template selection during filing']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.jurisdiction. Business justification: Court rules and procedural rules apply within specific legal jurisdictions. The court_rule table currently stores jurisdiction_scope as a denormalized STRING. Adding jurisdiction_id FK to matter.juris']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Court rules are stored as legal documents (local rules PDFs, standing orders). Linking court_rule to its stored document enables version-controlled access to rule text, supports deadline calculation v']
  parent_rule_court_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent rule if this is a sub-rule or subsection. Enables hierarchical rule structures (e.g., Rule 26(a)(1) is a child of Rule 26).']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Court rules vary by practice area (civil procedure, criminal, family, IP). Firms map rules to practice areas for compliance automation, deadline calculation engines, training programs, and knowledge m']
  primary_superseded_by_rule_court_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the court rule that supersedes or replaces this rule. Null if the rule is still active or was retired without replacement.']
  tribunal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the court or tribunal to which this rule applies. Links to the court master data.']
  advisory_committee_notes varchar [note: 'Summary or reference to advisory committee notes or official commentary explaining the purpose and application of this rule. Provides interpretive guidance.']
  amendment_history varchar [note: 'Summary of significant amendments to this rule, including dates and nature of changes. Supports historical research and transition planning.']
  applicable_case_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of case types to which this rule applies (e.g., civil, criminal, bankruptcy, family, probate). Null if the rule applies to all case types in the court.']
  citation_format varchar [note: 'The standard citation format for this rule as used in legal briefs and filings (e.g., Fed. R. Civ. P. 26(a), Local Rule 7.1(a)).']
  compliance_checklist varchar [note: 'Structured checklist or list of requirements that must be satisfied to comply with this rule. Supports automated compliance verification and attorney guidance.']
  court_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the court rule. Active rules are enforceable; superseded rules have been replaced; proposed rules are pending adoption.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this court rule record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  deadline_calculation_rule varchar [note: 'Formula or logic for calculating deadlines under this rule (e.g., 30 days from service, 14 days before hearing, within 21 days of order). Enables automated deadline tracking and calendar management.']
  ecf_event_code varchar [note: 'The ECF event code associated with filings governed by this rule. Used for automated docket entry and deadline calculation in CM/ECF systems.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this court rule became or will become effective and enforceable. Critical for determining applicability to pending matters.']
  electronic_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule mandates electronic filing through ECF (Electronic Case Filing) or similar systems. True if electronic filing is required; false if paper filing is permitted.']
  exceptions_and_exemptions varchar [note: 'Description of any exceptions, exemptions, or circumstances under which this rule does not apply or may be waived.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this court rule expires or was superseded. Null for rules that remain in effect indefinitely.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The date on which this court rule record was last reviewed or verified for accuracy and currency. Supports knowledge management and compliance assurance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this court rule record was last modified in the system. Audit trail field.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days notice required by this rule (e.g., 21 days notice for a motion hearing). Null if no specific notice period is mandated.']
  page_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pages allowed for filings governed by this rule. Null if no page limit applies.']
  penalty_for_noncompliance varchar [note: 'Description of sanctions, penalties, or consequences for failing to comply with this rule (e.g., dismissal, striking of pleadings, monetary sanctions, contempt).']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule by its primary procedural area. Enables filtering and compliance checks by rule type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filing|service|discovery|evidence|scheduling|motion_practice|case_management|electronic_filing|appeal|trial_procedure|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'The official alphanumeric code or citation identifier for the court rule (e.g., FRCP 26, Local Rule 7.1, CPR 3.9). This is the externally-known business identifier used in legal filings and references.']
  rule_text varchar [note: 'The full text of the court rule or a summary of its key provisions. May include subsections, clauses, and commentary.']
  rule_title varchar [note: 'The full official title or short description of the court rule (e.g., Duty to Disclose; General Provisions Governing Discovery, Motion Practice).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Distinguishes between federal/national rules, local court rules, standing orders, and practice directions. Determines hierarchy and applicability.']
  service_method_required varchar [note: 'The method of service required or permitted by this rule (e.g., personal service, certified mail, electronic service). Critical for compliance with service of process requirements.']
  source_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or hyperlink to the authoritative source document for this rule (e.g., court website, legal research platform, official government publication).']
  word_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of words allowed for filings governed by this rule. Null if no word limit applies.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of procedural rules, local rules, standing orders, and practice directions applicable to each court or tribunal. Stores rule code, rule title, effective date, expiry date, court reference, rule category (filing, service, discovery, evidence, scheduling), page/word limits, mandatory form references, and electronic filing requirements. Enables automated deadline calculation and filing compliance checks.'
}

Table matter.judgment {
  judgment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the judgment record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.trust_account. Business justification: Judgments often result in funds held in trust: settlement proceeds, damage awards, garnished funds, or judgment debtor payments. Attorneys must track which trust account holds judgment-related funds f']
  court_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the court that entered this judgment.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the court docket associated with this judgment.']
  escrow_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.escrow_arrangement. Business justification: Consent judgments and settlement judgments routinely establish or release escrow arrangements. Linking judgment to escrow_arrangement allows tracking of escrow funds tied to specific court judgments —']
  judge_id varchar [note: 'FK to court.judge']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter in which this judgment was entered.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.order. Business justification: Judgments are often based on or reference prior court orders. This FK allows tracking which order(s) led to or are associated with the judgment. The judgment table does not currently have this link, a']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_profile. Business justification: Judgments are client litigation outcomes. Direct link supports win/loss analysis, financial impact assessment, client satisfaction metrics, appeals planning, and enforcement tracking for judgment coll']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: A judgment in a regulatory enforcement matter directly resolves, modifies, or creates a regulatory obligation (e.g., consent decree imposing ongoing compliance requirements). Linking judgment to regul']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount awarded in the judgment, if applicable. Null for non-monetary judgments.']
  appeal_case_number varchar [note: 'Case number assigned by the appellate court for the appeal of this judgment.']
  appeal_court_name varchar [note: 'Name of the appellate court where an appeal was filed, if applicable.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Final date by which an appeal of this judgment must be filed.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which an appeal of this judgment was filed, if applicable.']
  attorney_fees_awarded decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of attorney fees awarded as part of the judgment, if applicable.']
  consent_judgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a consent judgment agreed to by all parties.']
  cost_award_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of court costs and litigation expenses awarded as part of the judgment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this judgment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the judgment amount.']
  declaratory_relief_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the judgment provides declaratory relief clarifying rights and obligations.']
  default_judgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this judgment was entered by default due to failure to respond or appear.']
  ecf_document_number varchar [note: 'Electronic Case Filing system document number for the judgment entry.']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of enforcement actions taken to collect or execute the judgment.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date on which the judgment was officially entered by the court.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the judgment was entered into the court record.']
  final_judgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a final judgment that disposes of all claims and parties.']
  for_client boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the judgment was favorable to the firm\'s client.']
  injunctive_relief_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the judgment includes injunctive relief or equitable remedies.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual interest rate applicable to the judgment amount from entry date until satisfaction, expressed as a percentage.']
  judgment_number varchar [note: 'Court-assigned judgment number or docket entry number for this judgment.']
  judgment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the judgment indicating its enforceability and procedural state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entered|appealed|affirmed|reversed|vacated|satisfied|enforced — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  judgment_text varchar [note: 'Full text of the judgment as entered by the court, or reference to the document location.']
  judgment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the judgment based on how it was obtained or entered.']
  lien_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a judgment lien has been filed to secure the judgment amount.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this judgment record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and commentary regarding the judgment, enforcement strategy, or related matters.']
  pacer_case_number varchar [note: 'PACER system case number for federal court electronic records access.']
  partial_judgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a partial judgment resolving some but not all claims.']
  prevailing_party varchar [note: 'Designation of which party prevailed in the judgment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: plaintiff|defendant|petitioner|respondent|claimant|cross_claimant|third_party — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prevailing_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party or parties that prevailed in the judgment.']
  satisfaction_date date [note: 'Date on which the judgment was satisfied or paid in full.']
  settlement_judgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this judgment reflects a court-entered settlement agreement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this judgment record originated.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the judgment\'s holdings, orders, and relief granted.']

  Note: 'Records final judgments, decrees, and court-entered settlements in litigated matters. Stores judgment type (default, consent, summary, trial verdict, appellate), entry date, court reference, docket reference, judgment amount, currency, prevailing party, interest rate, cost award, appeal deadline, satisfaction date, and enforcement status. Distinct from court_order — judgments are final dispositions that close or substantially resolve a docket.'
}

Table matter.matter {
  matter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the legal matter. Primary key. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT (matter is a long-running engagement container linking client, timekeepers, documents, and billing).']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: Every matter has a designated client-side contact responsible for receiving status updates, approving budgets, and signing off on strategy. This is a fundamental legal matter management requirement. m']
  engagement_scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.engagement_scope. Business justification: Matters operate under specific engagement scopes (panel appointments, rate agreements, authorized practice areas). This link enforces billing compliance (rate caps, LEDES requirements), validates matt']
  indemnity_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.indemnity_policy. Business justification: Matters are covered under specific professional indemnity policies based on incident date, practice area, and policy period. Claims management and regulatory compliance (SRA minimum terms compliance) ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.jurisdiction. Business justification: Every legal matter is governed by a specific legal jurisdiction (governing law). The matter table currently stores jurisdiction as a denormalized STRING. Adding jurisdiction_id FK to matter.jurisdicti']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.legal_service. Business justification: Matter opening requires classification against a service catalog entry to drive SLA obligations, conflict check requirements, KYC rules, billing code assignment, and service-line revenue reporting. Ev']
  outside_counsel_guideline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.outside_counsel_guideline. Business justification: Each matter must be governed by the client\'s OCG specifying rate caps, staffing restrictions, and budget approval thresholds. A direct FK enables matter-level OCG enforcement without joining through c']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Matters are categorized by practice area for resource allocation, billing rate determination, conflict checking scope, and management reporting. The practice_group attribute is a denormalized text fie']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client organization or individual for whom this matter is being handled. Links to the client master record.']
  referral_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.referral_source. Business justification: Referral fee management and business development ROI: law firms must track which referral source originated each matter to calculate referral fees, comply with referral agreements, and measure referra']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed schedule for invoicing the client. Determines when Work in Progress (WIP) is converted to invoices.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget for the matter in the firm\'s base currency. Used for budget tracking, variance analysis, and client communications. Confidential financial information.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date the matter was officially closed. Nullable for active matters. Once closed, no further time or disbursements may be posted without reopening.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the matter\'s confidentiality requirements. Ethical wall matters require strict information barriers to prevent conflicts of interest.']
  conflict_clearance_date date [note: 'Date the matter received conflict clearance approval. Required for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  conflict_cleared_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the matter has passed conflict checking and been approved for engagement. Must be true before work can commence.']
  court_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case number assigned by the court or tribunal for litigation matters. Used for Electronic Case Filing (ECF) and Public Access to Court Electronic Records (PACER) integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the matter record was first created in the practice management system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the matter\'s billing currency (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). All financial amounts are denominated in this currency.']
  engagement_letter_sent_date date [note: 'Date the formal engagement letter (LOE) was sent to the client. Required for professional indemnity insurance and regulatory compliance.']
  engagement_letter_signed_date date [note: 'Date the client signed and returned the engagement letter, formalizing the attorney-client relationship and scope of work.']
  estimated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total estimated billable hours required to complete the matter. Used for resource planning and Legal Project Management (LPM) tracking.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The billing model agreed with the client. Hourly is traditional time-based billing; AFA includes fixed fee, contingency, capped fee, and other non-hourly arrangements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|fixed_fee|contingency|blended_rate|capped_fee|success_fee|retainer|afa_other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'The body of law that governs the substantive legal issues in this matter (e.g., \'Delaware Corporate Law\', \'UK Employment Law\'). May differ from jurisdiction.']
  ledes_matter_category varchar [note: 'Standardized LEDES billing code category for the matter type (e.g., L01 for Litigation, L02 for Corporate). Enables electronic billing submission to corporate clients.']
  lpp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the matter and its documents are subject to Legal Professional Privilege (attorney-client privilege). Critical for eDiscovery and document production.']
  matter_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the matter scope, legal issues, and objectives. Confidential business information subject to Legal Professional Privilege (LPP).']
  matter_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name or title of the legal matter (e.g., \'Acme Corp v. Beta Inc. - Patent Infringement\', \'XYZ Merger and Acquisition\').']
  matter_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the matter, typically formatted as client code plus sequential number (e.g., CLI001-00123). Used in all client communications, invoices, and internal references.']
  matter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the matter. Active matters allow time entry and billing; closed matters are complete; archived matters are retained for compliance but inactive.']
  matter_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the matter by legal service category. Determines workflow, billing rules, and reporting groupings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: litigation|transactional|advisory|intellectual_property|regulatory_compliance|employment|real_estate|tax|bankruptcy|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified the matter record. Used for audit trails and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the matter record was last modified. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the firm office or location that opened and manages this matter (e.g., NYC, LON, SFO). Used for geographic profitability and resource planning.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date the matter was officially opened and approved for work to commence. Marks the start of the matter lifecycle and billing eligibility.']
  opposing_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the opposing party or counterparty in litigation or transactional matters. Used for conflict checking and matter identification. Confidential.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome or result of the matter upon closure. Used for knowledge management, precedent tracking, and performance analysis. Confidential.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Current phase or stage in the matter lifecycle. Used for Legal Project Management (LPM) tracking and resource forecasting. Phases vary by matter type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: intake|discovery|motion_practice|trial|appeal|settlement|closing|post_closing — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal risk assessment for the matter based on complexity, client profile, regulatory exposure, and reputational factors. Used for partner oversight and resource allocation. Confidential.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final settlement amount agreed in litigation or dispute resolution matters. Confidential financial information subject to non-disclosure agreements.']
  statute_of_limitations_date date [note: 'Critical deadline by which legal action must be filed or rights will be forfeited. Used for docket management and malpractice risk mitigation.']
  trial_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for trial or hearing in litigation matters. Nullable for non-litigation matters. Used for resource planning and deadline tracking.']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'Primary UTBMS task code representing the predominant activity type for this matter (e.g., A101 for Case Assessment, L110 for Legal Research). Used for client billing guidelines compliance.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every legal matter and case managed by the firm. Represents the central hub of the matter domain, capturing the full lifecycle from inception to closure. Stores matter number, matter name, practice group, matter type (litigation, transactional, advisory, IP, regulatory), open date, close date, status (active, suspended, closed, archived), responsible attorney, supervising partner, originating attorney, billing attorney, office, jurisdiction, governing law, matter description, LPM classification, budget amount, estimated hours, risk rating, confidentiality flag, LPP flag, and LEDES matter category. Sourced primarily from Elite 3E Client/Matter Management module.'
}

Table matter.phase {
  phase_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for phase']
  lpm_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.lpm_template. Business justification: Matter phases are instantiated from LPM templates that define standard milestones, budgets, task counts, and resource requirements. Legal project management depends on this link to track template util']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent matter to which this phase belongs. Links phase to the overarching legal matter or case.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.register. Business justification: Matter phases carry distinct risk profiles requiring phase-level risk register entries (e.g., discovery phase: e-discovery cost overrun risk; trial phase: adverse judgment risk). Phase-gate risk revie']
  sla_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_template. Business justification: Individual matter phases are governed by phase-specific SLA commitments (e.g., due diligence phase: 5-day turnaround). Linking phase to sla_template enables phase-level SLA breach monitoring, client r']
  actual_disbursements_to_date decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of actual disbursements (expenses) recorded against this phase to date. Used for budget vs. actual variance analysis.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date on which this phase was completed or closed. Null if phase is still in progress. Used for schedule variance analysis and audit trail.']
  actual_fees_to_date decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of actual professional fees (time charges) recorded against this phase to date. Calculated from time entries at applicable rates. Used for budget vs. actual variance analysis.']
  actual_hours_to_date decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of actual hours recorded against this phase to date. Sourced from time entry records. Used for budget vs. actual variance analysis.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date on which work on this phase commenced. Used for schedule variance analysis and audit trail.']
  budgeted_disbursements decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total disbursements (out-of-pocket expenses such as court fees, expert witness fees, travel, filing fees) allocated for this phase.']
  budgeted_fees decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total professional fees (in the matter currency) allocated for this phase. Includes attorney and paralegal time at standard or negotiated rates. Excludes disbursements.']
  budgeted_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated total hours allocated for this phase as part of the matter budget. Used for LPM planning and AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement) scoping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this phase record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this phase record is currently active and available for time entry and reporting. False for archived or logically deleted phases.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether time and expenses recorded against this phase are billable to the client. False for pro bono, internal, or non-billable phases.']
  is_contingent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this phase is subject to a contingent fee arrangement (fees payable only upon successful outcome). Common in litigation and personal injury matters.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this phase record was last updated. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing.']
  milestone_due_date date [note: 'Target due date for the key milestone or deliverable associated with this phase. Used for critical path tracking and client SLA management.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Optional name of a key milestone or deliverable associated with this phase (e.g., Summary Judgment Motion Filed, Closing Documents Executed). Used for client reporting and LPM tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this phase. Used for internal coordination, issue tracking, and knowledge capture.']
  phase_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope, objectives, and deliverables for this phase. Used for LPM (Legal Project Management) planning and client communication.']
  phase_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the phase (e.g., Pleadings, Discovery, Motion Practice, Trial, Appeal, Due Diligence, Negotiation, Closing). Describes the stage of work within the matter lifecycle.']
  phase_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the phase. Indicates whether the phase has not started, is actively in progress, is on hold pending external events, has been completed, was cancelled, or deferred to a later date.']
  phase_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the phase based on the nature of legal work: litigation (court-based dispute), transactional (M&A, corporate deals), advisory (counseling), compliance (regulatory adherence), IP prosecution (patent/trademark filing), dispute resolution (ADR, arbitration), regulatory (agency proceedings), or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: litigation|transactional|advisory|compliance|ip_prosecution|dispute_resolution|regulatory|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Target date by which this phase is planned to be completed. Used for LPM scheduling, milestone tracking, and client SLA (Service Level Agreement) management.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Target date on which this phase is planned to commence. Used for LPM scheduling and milestone tracking.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the risk level associated with this phase (e.g., risk of cost overrun, schedule delay, adverse outcome). Used for risk management and escalation.']
  sequence_order varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of the phase within the matter lifecycle. Used to display phases in chronological or logical sequence (e.g., 1=Intake, 2=Pleadings, 3=Discovery, 4=Trial).']

  Note: 'Defines the structured phases within a matter lifecycle used for LPM (Legal Project Management) and UTBMS-aligned task budgeting. Each phase record captures phase code, phase name (e.g., Pleadings, Discovery, Trial, Closing), sequence order, estimated hours, budgeted fees, actual hours to date, phase status, start date, target end date, and actual end date. Enables phase-level budget vs. actual tracking and milestone management across litigation and transactional matters.'
}

Table matter.task {
  task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the matter task record. Primary key.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Tasks often require specific client contact involvement (document requests, approval signatures, information gathering). This link enables targeted task assignment notifications, tracks client deliver']
  deadline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.matter_deadline. Business justification: Tasks may be created specifically to satisfy court-imposed deadlines tracked in matter_deadline. This FK allows linking tasks to the specific court deadlines they address, which is critical for litiga']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Matter tasks produce deliverable documents (drafted briefs, contracts, filings). Linking tasks to their output documents enables work product tracking, billing verification against deliverables, and c']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent matter to which this task belongs. Links task to the overarching legal matter or case.']
  mitigation_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_mitigation_action. Business justification: Risk mitigation actions often require specific matter tasks for implementation (e.g., policy updates, training, control testing). Links operational tasks to their originating risk mitigation requireme']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.obligation. Business justification: Tasks are created to fulfill specific contractual obligations — a named legal project management process. Linking task to obligation enables obligation fulfillment tracking, percentage completion repo']
  phase_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific phase within the matter lifecycle to which this task is assigned (e.g., discovery, trial preparation, closing).']
  predecessor_task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a prior task that must be completed before this task can begin. Supports task dependency modeling and critical path analysis in LPM workflows.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the task based on actual hours and timekeeper rates. Supports budget variance reporting and profitability analysis.']
  actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours recorded against this task by all timekeepers. Aggregated from time entry records for budget variance analysis and billing.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the task was formally approved. Null if approval is not required or task is not yet approved.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether task completion requires formal approval or review by a supervising attorney or partner before the task can be marked complete.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time and expenses incurred on this task are billable to the client. False for pro bono, internal administrative, or non-billable matter tasks.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the task is on hold or deferred. Captures dependencies, client delays, or external blockers.']
  budget_variance_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between estimated cost and actual cost (actual minus estimated). Positive values indicate over-budget, negative values indicate under-budget. Supports financial performance tracking.']
  budget_variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference between estimated hours and actual hours (actual minus estimated). Positive values indicate over-budget, negative values indicate under-budget. Supports budget performance analysis.']
  client_visible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task is visible to the client in client portal or matter status reports. False for internal administrative or strategy tasks.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date the task was marked complete. Null if task is not yet complete. Supports cycle time analysis and on-time delivery metrics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the task record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and task lifecycle tracking.']
  deliverable_description varchar [note: 'Description of the expected output or work product from this task (e.g., motion document, research memo, contract redline). Supports quality review and client communication.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the task. Used for deadline tracking, SLA monitoring, and LPM milestone management.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost for the task based on estimated hours and applicable timekeeper rates. Used for matter budget planning and AFA cost modeling.']
  estimated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned or budgeted hours to complete the task. Used for matter budgeting, workload forecasting, and Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) planning.']
  external_task_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier or reference number for the task in integrated systems (e.g., project management tool, client portal, court ECF system). Supports cross-system reconciliation.']
  is_milestone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task represents a key milestone in the matter lifecycle (e.g., complaint filed, discovery complete, trial date). Used for high-level matter status reporting and client communication.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the task record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments related to task execution, issues encountered, or coordination details. Supports knowledge capture and handoff continuity.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification for the task. Drives workload sequencing and resource allocation decisions.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the task was initiated or moved to in-progress status. Supports task duration and throughput analysis.']
  task_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the task type for reporting and workload analysis. Complements UTBMS task code with firm-specific categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: legal_research|document_drafting|client_communication|court_filing|discovery|negotiation|administrative — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the task scope, deliverables, and context. Supports LPM workflow documentation and task handoff clarity.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the task (e.g., Draft Motion to Dismiss, Review Contract Terms, Conduct Client Interview).']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the task. Tracks progression from assignment through completion or deferral.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'Standardized UTBMS task code for structured billing and matter budgeting (e.g., L110 for Case Assessment, L310 for Discovery). Enables LEDES-compliant billing narratives and industry-standard task classification.']

  Note: 'Granular task records within a matter phase, aligned to UTBMS task codes for structured billing and LPM tracking. Captures task code, task name, task description, assigned timekeeper, estimated hours, actual hours, task status (open, in-progress, complete, deferred), due date, completion date, priority level, and billable flag. Supports matter budgeting, workload distribution, and LEDES-compliant billing narratives. Sourced from Elite 3E task management and LPM workflow modules.'
}

Table matter.team {
  team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the matter team assignment record. Primary key for the matter team association entity.']
  conflict_clearance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the conflict clearance record that authorized this timekeeper\'s assignment to the matter. Links to Intapp Conflicts clearance record.']
  ethical_wall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to conflict.ethical_wall. Business justification: Team member assignments must enforce specific ethical walls. Linking team→ethical_wall enables direct system enforcement queries: \'which wall restricts this timekeeper on this matter?\' Team already ha']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter to which the timekeeper is assigned. Links to the matter record in Elite 3E or Aderant Expert.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the timekeeper\'s capacity allocated to this matter. Used for workload planning and resource management. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the timekeeper\'s assignment to the matter ended. Null if the timekeeper is still actively assigned to the matter.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the timekeeper\'s assignment to the matter. May include special instructions, scope limitations, or coordination notes.']
  assignment_source_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this assignment record in the source system (Elite 3E or Aderant Expert). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  assignment_source_system varchar [note: 'System from which the matter team assignment record originated. Values: elite_3e (Elite 3E Practice Management), aderant_expert (Aderant Expert Time and Billing), manual (manually entered), import (bulk import).']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the timekeeper was assigned to the matter team. Marks the beginning of the timekeeper\'s involvement in the matter.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the timekeeper\'s assignment to the matter. Values: active (currently working), inactive (assignment ended), suspended (temporarily paused), pending (assignment approved but not yet started), completed (matter closed, assignment complete).']
  billing_rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the billing rate override (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Aligns with the matter\'s billing currency.']
  billing_rate_override decimal(15,2) [note: 'Matter-specific billing rate override for this timekeeper, expressed in the matter\'s billing currency. Overrides the timekeeper\'s standard rate for this matter only. Used for Alternative Fee Arrangements (AFA) or negotiated rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this matter team assignment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the department or cost center to which the timekeeper\'s work on this matter is allocated. Used for internal financial reporting and profitability analysis.']
  ethical_wall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper is subject to an ethical wall (information barrier) for this matter due to conflict of interest considerations. True if ethical wall applies, False otherwise.']
  fee_budget_cap decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum fee amount this timekeeper is authorized to bill to the matter, expressed in the matter\'s billing currency. Used for Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) and budget governance. Null if no cap applies.']
  hours_budget_cap decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum number of hours this timekeeper is authorized to bill to the matter. Used for budget control and Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) compliance. Null if no cap applies.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the timekeeper is currently actively assigned to the matter. True if active, False if assignment has ended or is suspended.']
  is_billing_timekeeper boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper is authorized to approve billing and invoices for the matter. True if billing authority, False otherwise.']
  is_originating_timekeeper boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper originated the client relationship and matter. True if originating attorney, False otherwise. Used for credit allocation and Revenue Per Equity Partner (RPE) calculations.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timekeeper is the primary client contact for the matter. True if primary contact, False otherwise. Typically the Responsible Attorney.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this matter team assignment record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the office location from which the timekeeper is working on this matter. Used for geographic revenue allocation and cost center reporting.']
  practice_group_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the practice group under which this timekeeper is assigned to the matter (e.g., LIT for Litigation, CORP for Corporate, IP for Intellectual Property). May differ from the timekeeper\'s primary practice group.']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the timekeeper\'s role on the matter. Values: RESP_ATT (Responsible Attorney), SUPV_PRTNR (Supervising Partner), ASSOC (Associate), PARA (Paralegal), BILL_ATT (Billing Attorney), ORIG_ATT (Originating Attorney - client relationship owner), CO_COUNSEL (Co-Counsel), CONSULT (Consultant), EXPERT (Expert Witness), ADMIN (Administrative Support). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RESP_ATT|SUPV_PRTNR|ASSOC|PARA|BILL_ATT|ORIG_ATT|CO_COUNSEL|CONSULT|EXPERT|ADMIN — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  role_description varchar [note: 'Full text description of the timekeeper\'s role and responsibilities on the matter. Provides additional context beyond the role code.']

  Note: 'Association entity capturing the assignment of timekeepers and staff to a matter, representing the matter team roster. Records timekeeper role on the matter (responsible attorney, supervising partner, associate, paralegal, billing attorney, originating attorney, co-counsel), assignment start date, assignment end date, billing rate override, hours cap, and active flag. Enables matter-level staffing management, rate governance, and workload reporting. Sourced from Elite 3E and Aderant Expert timekeeper-matter assignment modules.'
}

Table matter.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the matter budget record. Primary key.']
  fee_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.fee_arrangement. Business justification: Budget vs. billing arrangement variance reporting is a core financial management process in legal operations. Firms compare matter budget performance (internal cost planning) against fee arrangement c']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent matter for which this budget and rate schedule applies.']
  outside_counsel_guideline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.outside_counsel_guideline. Business justification: Matter budgets must be validated against OCG rate caps, budget approval thresholds, and billing format requirements. Legal operations and billing teams require this direct link to enforce OCG constrai']
  phase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to matter.phase. Business justification: Budgets are often allocated at the phase level for legal project management. The budget table has phase_name and phase_code as STRING attributes but no FK to the phase entity. Adding this FK allows pr']
  pricing_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.pricing_model. Business justification: Matter budgets implement specific pricing models (hourly, fixed-fee, contingency, blended rate, retainer). Budget approval workflows validate budget structure against pricing model parameters. The afa']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.rate_card. Business justification: Budgets are constructed from rate cards that define approved hourly rates and billing parameters. Budget approval, variance analysis, and rate compliance reporting all require knowing which rate card ']
  retainer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.retainer_agreement. Business justification: Matter budgets are funded by retainer agreements; linking budget to retainer_agreement enables real-time comparison of approved budget against available retainer balance. Legal finance teams use this ']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget amount for the matter or phase, expressed in the budget currency.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the client or engagement partner formally approved this budget version.']
  budget_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the matter budget, typically used in engagement letters and client communications.']
  budget_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the budget: engagement letter, LOE (Letter of Engagement), RFP (Request for Proposal) response, court order (fee cap), client guideline (outside counsel guideline), or internal estimate.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget: draft (under preparation), approved (client-accepted), revised (amended post-approval), closed (matter concluded), suspended (on hold), or exceeded (over budget threshold).']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget scope: total fees (all professional fees), phase fees (by litigation or transaction phase), disbursements (out-of-pocket costs), total matter (fees plus disbursements), task-based (UTBMS task codes), or milestone-based (deliverable-driven).']
  client_agreed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has formally agreed to this budget in writing (engagement letter, LOE, or budget approval email).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this budget version becomes effective for WIP (Work in Progress) valuation and billing.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this budget version expires or is superseded by a new version. Null for open-ended budgets.']
  hourly_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly billing rate for the timekeeper or role, expressed in the rate currency. Null for non-hourly AFA arrangements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing budget assumptions, client instructions, special terms, or variance explanations.']
  rate_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the rate: draft (under negotiation), approved (finalized), pending_client_approval (awaiting client sign-off), rejected (client declined), or superseded (replaced by newer rate).']
  rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the hourly rate amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  rate_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this rate becomes effective for time entry valuation and billing.']
  rate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this rate expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended rate agreements.']
  rate_schedule_name varchar [note: 'Name of the rate schedule or rate card associated with this budget (e.g., Standard 2024, Client ABC Preferred Rates, AFA Blended Rate).']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rate: engagement letter (LOE-specified rate), rate_schedule (firm rate card), court_order (court-imposed fee cap or rate), client_guideline (outside counsel guideline rate), or firm_standard (default firm rate).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing rate: standard (firm standard rate), agreed (client-negotiated rate), afa_blended (single blended rate for AFA), pro_bono (zero rate for public interest work), discounted (reduced rate), or premium (elevated rate for specialized work).']
  rate_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rate to track rate changes and amendments over the matter lifecycle.']
  timekeeper_role varchar [note: 'Role or level of the timekeeper for rate governance (e.g., Equity Partner, Non-Equity Partner, Senior Associate, Associate, Paralegal, Legal Assistant). Used for role-based rate schedules.']
  variance_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage threshold above which budget variance triggers client notification or approval requirement (e.g., 10.00 means notify at 10% over budget).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the budget to track revisions and amendments over the matter lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Formal matter budget, fee arrangement, and rate governance record serving as the single source of truth for all financial planning, approved fee budgets, cost budgets, and matter-level billing rate agreements. Supports AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement) and hourly billing models. Stores budget type (total fees, phase fees, disbursements, total matter), budget amount, currency, approved date, approved by, budget version, budget status (draft, approved, revised, closed), AFA type (fixed fee, capped fee, blended rate, contingency, success fee), variance threshold percentage. Includes comprehensive rate schedule management: timekeeper role, individual timekeeper reference, rate type (standard, agreed, AFA blended, pro bono), hourly rate amount, rate currency, effective date, expiry date, rate approval status, client-agreed flag, rate source (engagement letter, rate schedule, court order), and rate version history. Enables WIP monitoring, budget vs. actual reporting, rate card management, client budget compliance, LEDES-compliant billing, rate benchmarking analytics, and accurate WIP valuation. This product is the single home for all matter-level rate governance — there is no separate rate entity. Sourced from Elite 3E and Aderant Expert rate and budget management modules.'
}

  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party\'s role in the matter. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: plaintiff|defendant|counterparty|guarantor|regulator|witness|expert|joint_venture_partner|licensor|licensee — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pep_status varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the party is a politically exposed person. Used for enhanced due diligence in AML compliance.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code component of the party\'s primary address.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or incorporation number assigned by the jurisdiction. Used for corporate entities to verify legal standing.']
  representation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the party is represented by legal counsel, unrepresented, self-represented, or representation status is pending determination.']
  representing_counsel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary attorney or counsel representing this party. Null if party is unrepresented.']
  representing_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the law firm or legal organization representing this party in the matter. Null if party is unrepresented.']
  role_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the party\'s role in the matter became effective. Marks the beginning of the party\'s involvement in the legal matter.']
  role_end_date date [note: 'Date when the party\'s role in the matter ended. Null for parties still actively involved. Populated when party is dismissed, settled, or withdrawn.']
  sanctions_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for this party. Indicates whether party appears on government sanctions lists or watchlists.']
  settlement_authority_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum settlement amount authorized for this party. Used in litigation and dispute resolution to track negotiation parameters.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the party\'s primary address. Uses standard state/province codes where applicable.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number for the party. May be SSN, EIN, VAT number, or other jurisdiction-specific tax identifier. Critical for corporate transactions and tax reporting.']

  Note: 'All parties to a legal matter beyond the primary client, including adverse parties, co-parties, third parties, intervenors, guarantors, counterparties in transactions, joint venture partners, regulatory bodies, government agencies, and beneficial owners. Captures party name, party type (plaintiff, defendant, counterparty, guarantor, regulator, witness, expert, joint venture partner, licensor, licensee), entity type (individual, corporation, partnership, trust, government), jurisdiction of incorporation or domicile, representation status, party role effective date, party status (active, settled, dismissed, withdrawn), and KYC/AML screening reference. Critical for conflict checking, litigation management, corporate transaction advisory, and regulatory filing party disclosures.'
}
