  utbms_expense_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS expense code applicable when the time entry is associated with a disbursement or expense component (e.g., E101 for Copying, E106 for Computer Research). Nullable for pure time entries with no expense component.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS standardized task code classifying the type of legal work performed (e.g., L110 for Fact Investigation, L120 for Analysis/Strategy, B110 for Retention/Billing). Required for LEDES electronic billing submissions and task-based matter management reporting.']
  wip_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The monetary value of unbilled work represented by this time entry, calculated as billable_hours multiplied by billing_rate. Represents the WIP balance contribution of this entry. Reduced to zero when the entry is billed or written off.']
  work_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the legal work was actually performed. This is the principal business event date for the time entry, distinct from the date the entry was created or submitted in the system. Critical for WIP aging, billing period cutoffs, and matter chronology.']
  write_down_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The monetary amount by which the original WIP value of this entry was reduced during prebilling review or billing adjustment. Represents the difference between the originally recorded WIP value and the amount actually billed. Used for realization rate analysis and partner performance reporting.']
  write_down_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for any write-down applied to this time entry (e.g., client discount, billing guideline non-compliance, courtesy reduction, duplicate entry). Required for realization analysis and billing adjustment reporting.']

  Note: 'Granular transactional record of billable and non-billable time recorded by timekeepers against a matter. Captures UTBMS task and activity codes, narrative descriptions, hours worked, billable hours, billing rate at time of entry, WIP value, and timekeeper identity. Supports LEDES export format for electronic billing. Tracks entry status through draft, submitted, approved, billed, and written-off lifecycle stages. Primary source record for WIP accumulation and invoice generation.'
}

Table billing.billing_disbursement {
  billing_disbursement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each disbursement record in the billing domain. Primary key for the disbursement data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.asset. Business justification: IP-specific disbursements (filing fees, annuity payments, prosecution expenses, translation costs) are directly attributable to specific IP assets for cost recovery, portfolio profitability analysis, ']
  docket_deadline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.docket_deadline. Business justification: Annuity payments and official fee disbursements are triggered by docket deadlines. Linking disbursement to the deadline that triggered it supports IP compliance cost reporting and confirms that deadli']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Court-related expenses (filing fees, service costs, transcript fees) are tracked against specific dockets for client billing, cost recovery, and LEDES expense reporting in litigation matters.']
  fee_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.fee_arrangement. Business justification: Disbursements occur within the context of a fee arrangement, which governs whether expenses are billable, subject to caps, or excluded. Currently disbursement has fee_arrangement_type (string code) bu']
  filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.filing. Business justification: Filing fees are direct costs tied to specific court filings. Firms track disbursements per filing for accurate cost recovery, client transparency, and LEDES compliance.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client matter against which this disbursement is recorded. Links the disbursement to the matter for Work in Progress (WIP) accumulation and billing purposes.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Disbursements are tracked by practice area for expense policy compliance, reimbursement approval, and profitability analysis. Practice area determines expense policies, markup rules, and cost manageme']
  production_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.production_set. Business justification: Document productions incur disbursements (printing, media, delivery, processing vendor fees) tracked at the production set level. Firms bill clients for production costs per set. A litigation billing ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client on whose behalf the disbursement was incurred. Used for client-level cost reporting and billing aggregation.']
  prosecution_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.prosecution_event. Business justification: Official fees and filing disbursements are incurred at specific prosecution events (filing responses, paying issue fees). IP cost tracking and client billing require linking each disbursement to the p']
  trust_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.account. Business justification: Billing disbursements (court fees, filing costs) are frequently funded directly from client trust accounts. Regulatory compliance and three-way reconciliation require tracing each trust-funded disburs']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross amount of the disbursement as incurred or paid, before any tax, markup, or write-down adjustments. Represents the base cost for WIP accumulation and billing calculations.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the partner or billing supervisor who approved the disbursement for billing. Required for disbursements above the firm\'s approval threshold and for audit trail purposes.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the disbursement was approved for billing by the responsible partner or billing supervisor. Used for billing workflow tracking and audit compliance.']
  base_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The disbursement amount converted to the firm\'s base billing currency using the applicable exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting, Profit Per Partner (PPP) and Revenue Per Equity Partner (RPE) KPI calculations.']
  billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount actually billed to the client on the invoice, after applying any markup, write-down, or billing adjustment. May differ from the disbursement amount if a write-down was applied during prebilling review.']
  billing_disbursement_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the disbursement as it will appear on the client invoice and in the billing system. Describes the nature of the expense incurred (e.g., \'Court filing fee — Superior Court of California\', \'Expert witness fee — Dr. J. Smith\'). Supports LEDES line-item narrative requirements.']
  billing_period varchar [note: 'The billing period (YYYY-MM) to which the disbursement is allocated for invoicing purposes. Used for period-based WIP reporting, prebilling, and financial close processes.']
  cost_centre_code varchar [note: 'The internal cost centre code to which the disbursement is attributed for management accounting and profitability reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the disbursement record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, WIP aging, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the disbursement amount is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency matters and cross-border transactions.']
  disbursement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the disbursement record. Used for audit trails, client queries, and cross-system reconciliation with Elite 3E and Aderant Expert.']
  disbursement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the disbursement record. \'wip\' indicates the cost is in Work in Progress (WIP) awaiting billing; \'billed\' indicates it has been included on a client invoice; \'written_off\' indicates the amount has been written off and will not be recovered.']
  disbursement_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the disbursement as a hard disbursement (direct out-of-pocket cost paid to a third party on behalf of the client, e.g., court filing fees, expert witness fees) or a soft disbursement (internal overhead recovery, e.g., photocopying, internal printing). Determines pass-through billing treatment.']
  entry_date date [note: 'The date on which the disbursement was entered into the practice management system. May differ from the expense date when costs are posted retrospectively. Used for WIP aging calculations from the entry date perspective.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the disbursement amount from the transaction currency to the firm\'s base billing currency at the time of entry. Used for multi-currency matter billing and financial consolidation.']
  expense_category varchar [note: 'Internal firm classification of the disbursement type used for cost management and reporting (e.g., court_filing, expert_witness, travel, courier, photocopying, translation, process_server, deposition). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: court_filing|expert_witness|travel|courier|photocopying|translation|process_server|deposition|search_fee|recording_fee — promote to reference product]']
  expense_date date [note: 'The real-world date on which the disbursement was incurred or the expense was paid. This is the principal business event date used for WIP aging, billing period allocation, and financial reporting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger (GL) account code to which the disbursement is posted in the firm\'s accounting system. Used for financial reporting, cost allocation, and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) compliance.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disbursement is billable to the client (True) or is a non-billable internal cost (False). Non-billable disbursements are absorbed by the firm and excluded from client invoices but included in matter cost reporting.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disbursement is subject to applicable taxes (e.g., VAT, GST) when billed to the client. Drives tax calculation logic in the billing system.']
  ledes_expense_code varchar [note: 'The Legal Electronic Data Exchange Standard (LEDES) expense code used in electronic billing submissions to corporate clients and insurance companies. Aligns with LEDES 1998B and LEDES XML format requirements.']
  markup_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage markup applied to the base disbursement amount when billing to the client. Common for soft disbursements such as photocopying or printing where the firm applies a standard recovery rate above cost. Zero for hard disbursements passed through at cost.']
  narrative_for_client varchar [note: 'The client-facing narrative or description of the disbursement as it appears on the client invoice. May differ from the internal description field. Supports compliance with client billing guidelines and Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) reporting requirements.']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the firm office or location from which the disbursement was incurred. Used for geographic cost allocation, multi-office financial reporting, and jurisdictional tax compliance.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity or unit count associated with the disbursement where applicable (e.g., number of pages photocopied, number of miles travelled, number of documents served). Used with unit_rate to derive the disbursement amount for volume-based expenses.']
  receipt_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the receipt or proof of payment supporting the disbursement. Used for expense substantiation, audit compliance, and client billing dispute resolution.']
  recovery_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current recovery status of the disbursement cost from the client. \'unrecovered\' means the cost has not yet been billed or paid; \'partially_recovered\' means a portion has been collected; \'fully_recovered\' means the full amount has been received; \'written_off\' means recovery has been abandoned.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system from which the disbursement record was sourced (e.g., Elite 3E, Aderant Expert, manual entry, expense portal). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax) applicable to the disbursement as billed to the client. Calculated based on the applicable tax code and jurisdiction. Required for tax compliance reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to the disbursement to determine the applicable tax rate and treatment (e.g., VAT standard rate, zero-rated, exempt). Sourced from the firm\'s tax configuration in Elite 3E.']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The per-unit rate applied to the quantity to calculate the disbursement amount (e.g., cost per page, cost per mile). Used for volume-based soft disbursements such as photocopying and mileage recovery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the disbursement record was last modified in the source system. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental load processing.']
  utbms_expense_code varchar [note: 'Standardised Uniform Task-Based Management System (UTBMS) expense code classifying the nature of the disbursement (e.g., E101 for copying, E106 for computer research, E112 for court fees). Required for LEDES electronic billing submissions to corporate clients.']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The invoice or reference number from the third-party vendor\'s invoice supporting the disbursement. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and audit trail purposes.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party vendor, supplier, or service provider from whom the disbursement cost was incurred (e.g., court, expert witness firm, courier company, translation agency). Applicable primarily to hard disbursements.']
  write_down_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount by which the disbursement was reduced during prebilling review before invoicing. Represents the difference between the recorded amount and the billed amount. Used for realization rate analysis and profitability reporting.']
  write_off_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the disbursement was written off or written down. Used for profitability analysis, billing quality management, and partner performance reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of out-of-pocket expenses and costs incurred on behalf of a client matter, including court filing fees, expert witness fees, travel, courier, photocopying, and third-party vendor costs. Captures UTBMS expense codes, disbursement type, amount, tax treatment, billable flag, and recovery status. Supports both hard disbursements (direct client pass-through costs) and soft disbursements (overhead recoveries). Feeds WIP accumulation alongside time entries.'
}

Table billing.wip_ledger {
  wip_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each WIP ledger entry. Primary key for the wip_ledger data product in the billing domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.ip_asset. Business justification: IP portfolio WIP aging reports require WIP ledger entries to be tracked per IP asset. Law firms run per-asset WIP reports to identify unbilled prosecution costs and manage IP portfolio billing cycles.']
  billing_disbursement_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.disbursement.disbursement_id — WIP ledger entries also accumulate from disbursements. This link enables complete WIP composition analysis showing both time and expense components.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: WIP accumulates against litigation matters with dockets. Essential for matter-level WIP aging, prebilling review, litigation profitability tracking, and partner oversight of court-related unbilled wor']
  fee_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.fee_arrangement. Business justification: WIP accumulates under a specific fee arrangement, which determines billing rules, rate structures, and conversion to invoices. Currently wip_ledger has fee_arrangement_type (string) but no FK to the a']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: WIP aging and realization reports in legal firms are always segmented by practice area for partner compensation and profitability analysis. Legal finance teams run WIP-by-practice-area reports for mon']
  primary_wip_matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter against which this WIP entry is recorded. Matters are the primary billing unit in legal practice management.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client associated with the matter for which WIP is being tracked. Used in client-level WIP reporting and PPP/PEP calculations.']
  trust_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.account. Business justification: WIP entries funded from or converted via trust (earned-fee transfers from trust-to-office) must reference the source trust account. Supports WIP-to-trust reconciliation and earned-fee transfer audit t']
  wip_time_entry_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.time_entry.time_entry_id — WIP ledger entries accumulate from time entries. This link enables WIP drill-down to source time records and supports WIP aging analysis at the entry level.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Categorical aging classification of the WIP entry based on wip_age_days. Used in WIP aging reports for financial management, partner reviews, and collections strategy. Buckets align with standard legal industry WIP aging reporting conventions.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the WIP entry was approved for billing or the write-off/write-down was authorised. Used in billing workflow tracking and SOX compliance audit trails.']
  billing_office_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the firm office responsible for billing this WIP entry. Used for office-level revenue attribution, PPP/PEP calculations by office, and financial reporting segmentation.']
  billing_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard billing rate per hour applied to this WIP entry, expressed in the matter currency. Reflects the timekeeper\'s rate for the applicable billing period and client rate agreement. Confidential as it reflects negotiated client pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this WIP ledger record was first created in the source practice management system. Provides the authoritative record creation audit timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this WIP entry (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency billing for international matters and cross-border client engagements.']
  entry_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the WIP entry was recorded in the practice management system. May differ from work_date due to late time entry. Used for compliance monitoring of timekeeping policies and WIP aging from entry perspective.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the WIP entry amount from the matter currency to the firm\'s functional reporting currency at the time of entry. Used for consolidated financial reporting and multi-currency WIP analysis.']
  hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of billable hours recorded for this WIP entry. Applicable when wip_type is \'time\'. Expressed in decimal hours (e.g., 1.50 = 1 hour 30 minutes). Null for disbursement and fixed-fee entries.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this WIP entry is designated as billable to the client (True) or non-billable/internal (False). Non-billable WIP is tracked for cost management and matter profitability analysis but excluded from client invoices.']
  is_contingency_accrual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this WIP entry represents an accrued contingency fee amount (True) that is not yet realised pending case outcome. Contingency WIP is tracked separately for financial reporting and is only recognised as revenue upon successful resolution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this WIP ledger record was most recently updated in the source practice management system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver layer.']
  phase_code varchar [note: 'Matter phase code identifying the stage of the legal engagement to which this WIP entry belongs (e.g., pleadings, discovery, trial). Used in matter budgeting, phase-based billing, and LPM (Legal Project Management) reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this WIP ledger entry was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability in the Silver layer of the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  standard_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The WIP value calculated at the timekeeper\'s standard rack rate (hours_worked multiplied by standard_rate). Used to measure the variance between standard and billed value, supporting write-up/write-down analysis and profitability reporting.']
  standard_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The timekeeper\'s published standard rack rate per hour before any client-specific discounts or negotiated rate agreements are applied. Used to calculate write-up/write-down variance against billing_rate for financial KPI reporting including RPE.']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS standardised activity code describing the specific activity performed within a task (e.g., A101 for Plan and Prepare, A102 for Research). Complements the task code for granular task-based billing and client invoice compliance.']
  utbms_expense_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS standardised expense code for disbursement and expense WIP entries (e.g., E101 for Copying, E106 for Online Research). Populated only when wip_type is \'disbursement\' or \'expense\'.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS standardised task code classifying the legal work performed (e.g., L110 for Fact Investigation, L120 for Analysis/Strategy). Enables task-based billing analysis, client reporting, and benchmarking against industry spend data.']
  wip_accumulates_disbursement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.disbursement.disbursement_id — WIP ledger entries also accumulate from disbursements. This FK enables complete WIP composition analysis.']
  wip_age_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed since the work_date of this WIP entry. Used to populate WIP aging buckets (0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days) for financial reporting, collections prioritisation, and partner performance reviews.']
  wip_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross WIP value of this entry calculated as hours_worked multiplied by billing_rate for time entries, or the disbursement amount for expense entries. Represents the unbilled revenue value before any write-up or write-down adjustments. Core field for PPP, PEP, and RPE financial KPI calculations.']
  wip_amount_firm_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The WIP amount converted to the firm\'s functional reporting currency using the exchange_rate. Enables consolidated WIP reporting, PPP, PEP, and RPE calculations across matters billed in multiple currencies.']
  wip_entry_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this WIP ledger entry as assigned by the practice management system (e.g., Elite 3E transaction number). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trails.']
  wip_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the WIP ledger entry. \'open\' indicates unbilled WIP available for prebilling; \'billed\' indicates the entry has been included on an invoice; \'written_off\' indicates the amount has been written off; \'transferred\' indicates the WIP has been moved to another matter; \'on_hold\' indicates billing is suspended; \'reversed\' indicates the entry has been reversed.']
  wip_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the WIP entry by nature of the charge. \'time\' represents billable hours; \'disbursement\' represents client-chargeable out-of-pocket costs; \'expense\' represents firm expenses; \'fixed_fee\' represents flat-fee milestone charges; \'contingency\' represents contingency-based accruals.']
  work_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the billable work or disbursement was performed. This is the principal real-world event date for the WIP entry, distinct from the date the entry was recorded in the system. Critical for WIP aging calculations and billing period allocation.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the work performed or disbursement incurred as entered by the timekeeper. Appears on prebill and client invoice. Subject to billing guidelines review and may be edited during the prebilling process.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of WIP that has been written off and will not be billed to the client. Populated when wip_status is \'written_off\'. Used in realisation rate calculations and partner performance reviews.']
  write_off_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised reason code explaining why the WIP entry was written off (e.g., client dispute, fee cap exceeded, courtesy write-off, uncollectable). Populated when wip_status is \'written_off\'. Used in write-off analysis and partner performance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: client_dispute|fee_cap_exceeded|courtesy|uncollectable|billing_error|matter_closed — promote to reference product]']
  write_up_down_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary adjustment applied to the WIP entry representing the difference between the standard amount and the billed amount. Positive values indicate a write-up (billing above standard); negative values indicate a write-down (billing below standard). Critical for realisation rate analysis and partner performance reporting.']

  Note: 'Accumulated Work in Progress ledger representing unbilled time and disbursements for each matter. Tracks WIP balance by timekeeper, matter, billing period, and fee arrangement type. Provides the authoritative WIP snapshot used in prebilling and financial reporting. Captures WIP aging buckets, write-up/write-down adjustments, and transfer history. Critical for PPP (Profit Per Partner), PEP (Profit Per Equity Partner), and RPE (Revenue Per Employee) financial KPI calculations.'
}

Table billing.prebill {
  prebill_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the prebill record. Primary key for the prebill data product in the billing domain.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Prebills for litigation matters reference specific dockets for attorney review of court-related charges, billing guideline compliance checking, and client communication before final invoicing.']
  fee_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee arrangement governing this prebill, such as hourly, flat fee, contingency, or Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA). Determines billing calculation methodology.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the final invoice generated from this approved prebill. Populated once the prebill transitions to approved status and the invoice is created. Null if the prebill has not yet been converted to an invoice.']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.legal_service. Business justification: Prebills are generated for work performed on a specific legal service. Legal billing teams review prebills by service type for service-level revenue recognition and billing guideline compliance. prebi']
  letter_of_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.letter_of_engagement. Business justification: Prebill review validates proposed charges against the letter of engagement terms before invoice finalization. Billing attorneys must verify scope compliance, rate adherence, and guideline conformance ']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Prebills are reviewed and approved by practice area leadership. Practice area determines approval authority, billing guideline compliance rules, and realization targets used in prebill review workflow']
  prebill_ledes_matter_id varchar [note: 'Client-assigned matter identifier formatted per LEDES electronic billing standards. Required for electronic invoice submission to corporate clients and insurance carriers using LEDES-compliant billing systems.']
  prebill_matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter against which this prebill is generated. Links the prebill to the specific legal matter or case being billed.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity responsible for payment of this prebill. Derived from the matter-client relationship for billing purposes.']
  wip_ledger_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.wip_ledger.wip_ledger_id — Prebills are generated from WIP balances. This link enables the prebilling workflow to pull unbilled WIP for attorney review and ensures WIP-to-invoice traceability.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing attorney formally approved the prebill for invoice generation. Marks the transition to approved status and triggers the invoice creation workflow.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which invoices are issued for this matter, as agreed in the engagement letter. Drives prebill generation scheduling: monthly, quarterly, at defined milestones, on-demand, or annually.']
  billing_guideline_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the prebill has passed automated billing guideline compliance checks against the applicable client billing guidelines. True = compliant; False = one or more violations detected requiring attorney review.']
  billing_guideline_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the client billing guidelines applied during prebill review and compliance checking. Corporate clients and insurers issue versioned billing guidelines that restrict rates, timekeepers, task codes, and narrative formats.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the billing period covered by this prebill. Defines the inclusive end of the Work in Progress (WIP) window extracted for review.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'First date of the billing period covered by this prebill. Defines the inclusive start of the Work in Progress (WIP) window extracted for review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the prebill record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this prebill (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency billing for international matters.']
  disbursement_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net adjustment applied to disbursements on this prebill. Positive values indicate write-up; negative values indicate write-down or non-billable disbursement exclusions.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing fee structure applied to this prebill. Determines how WIP is calculated and presented: hourly (time-based), flat_fee (fixed amount), contingency (outcome-based percentage), afa (Alternative Fee Arrangement), blended_rate (composite rate across timekeepers), retainer (periodic fixed fee). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|flat_fee|contingency|afa|blended_rate|retainer — promote to reference product]']
  fee_writedown_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Negative adjustment applied to WIP fees to reduce the billed amount below the standard rate value. Reflects billing guideline compliance, client relationship management, or billing attorney discretion. Stored as a positive number representing the reduction.']
  fee_writeup_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Positive adjustment applied to WIP fees to increase the billed amount above the standard rate value. Reflects premium billing for exceptional work, complexity, or successful outcomes. A zero value indicates no write-up was applied.']
  generated_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the prebill draft was generated from the WIP ledger. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the prebill lifecycle.']
  guideline_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual billing guideline violations detected on this prebill during automated compliance checking. A count of zero indicates full compliance. Used to prioritize prebill review effort.']
  narrative_edited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any time entry or disbursement narrative was edited during the prebill review process. True = narratives were modified; False = original narratives retained. Supports billing quality audit and Legal Professional Privilege (LPP) review.']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the firm office responsible for this prebill. Supports revenue allocation, profitability reporting, and Revenue Per Equity Partner (RPE) calculations at the office level.']
  on_account_credit_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of retainer or on-account funds applied against this prebill to reduce the net amount due. Relevant for matters operating under a retainer or advance payment arrangement. Zero if no on-account funds are applied.']
  prebill_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for the prebill draft. Used by billing staff and attorneys to reference the prebill during review and approval workflows. Format: PB-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  prebill_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the prebill in the billing quality control process. Drives routing logic: draft (initial WIP extraction), under_review (sent to billing attorney), approved (ready for invoice generation), rejected (returned for correction), on_hold (pending client instruction), cancelled (abandoned). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|rejected|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  prior_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding accounts receivable balance from prior invoices for this matter as of the prebill generation date. Displayed on the prebill for attorney awareness and collections follow-up context.']
  proposed_disbursements_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net disbursement amount proposed for invoicing after applying disbursement adjustments. Represents the billable expense total to be carried forward to the final invoice.']
  proposed_fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net fee amount proposed for invoicing after applying write-up and write-down adjustments to WIP fees. Equals wip_fees_amount plus fee_writeup_amount minus fee_writedown_amount.']
  proposed_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total proposed invoice value combining adjusted fees and adjusted disbursements. This is the gross amount that will appear on the final invoice if the prebill is approved without further changes.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the reviewing attorney when rejecting the prebill. Documents the basis for rejection (e.g., narrative edits required, rate corrections needed, time entries disputed) to guide billing staff corrections.']
  sent_for_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prebill was dispatched to the billing attorney for review. Marks the transition from draft to under_review status. Used to measure prebill review cycle time.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this prebill record was extracted. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer. Expected values: elite_3e (Elite 3E Practice Management), aderant_expert (Aderant Expert Time and Billing).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Applicable tax (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax) calculated on the proposed billable amounts. Jurisdiction-dependent; may be zero for exempt matters or jurisdictions where legal services are not taxable.']
  timekeeper_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct timekeepers (attorneys, paralegals, legal assistants) whose time entries are included in this prebill. Used for staffing analysis and billing guideline compliance (e.g., restrictions on number of timekeepers per matter).']
  total_hours_billed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Aggregate billable hours included in this prebill across all timekeepers and time entries within the billing period. Applicable for hourly and blended-rate fee arrangements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the prebill record. Tracks changes during the review, editing, and approval workflow.']
  wip_disbursements_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross value of unbilled disbursement and expense entries extracted for this prebill before any adjustments. Includes hard costs (court fees, filing fees, expert witness fees) and soft costs (photocopying, postage).']
  wip_fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross value of unbilled fee time entries (Work in Progress) extracted for this prebill before any adjustments. Represents the raw billable fees from timekeeper entries within the billing period.']
  wip_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Combined gross value of all WIP fees and disbursements extracted for this prebill prior to any write-up, write-down, or adjustment. Equals wip_fees_amount plus wip_disbursements_amount.']

  Note: 'Pre-invoice draft generated from WIP for attorney review and approval before final invoice issuance. Captures prebill number, matter reference, billing attorney, billing period, total WIP value, proposed adjustments, write-up/write-down amounts, and prebill status (draft, under review, approved, rejected). Supports the prebilling workflow including partner review, narrative editing, and billing guideline compliance checks. Central to the legal billing quality control process.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the invoice record in the billing system. Primary key for the invoice data product in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_agreement. Business justification: Invoices in legal services are directly governed by a contract agreement that sets payment terms, billing frequency, LEDES format, and dispute resolution method. Direct FK enables invoice-level contra']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Invoices bill work performed on litigation matters with dockets. Critical for client billing, LEDES submission, litigation cost tracking, and matter-level revenue recognition in court cases.']
  fee_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.fee_arrangement.fee_arrangement_id — Invoices are generated under the terms of a fee arrangement. This FK enables fee cap compliance checking and AFA billing validation.']
  governing_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.amendment. Business justification: Post-amendment invoices must reference the governing amendment to ensure correct rate application and billing guideline version compliance. Legal e-billing audits and client disputes frequently requir']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.legal_service. Business justification: Invoices are issued for a specific legal service delivered. Legal finance teams report revenue by service type for service profitability analysis and client billing transparency. practice_area is alre']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary matter for which legal services are billed. A single invoice may span multiple matters; this captures the primary or controlling matter.']
  originating_invoice_id varchar [note: 'For credit notes and adjustment invoices, the invoice_id of the original invoice being credited, reversed, or adjusted. Null for standard invoices. Enables full credit note lineage tracing.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Invoices are analyzed by practice area for revenue recognition, realization reporting, and profitability analysis. Practice area is the primary dimension for financial reporting, partner compensation,']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity to whom this invoice is addressed. Supports client-level AR aging, collections, and revenue reporting.']
  amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount received against this invoice to date. Used to calculate the outstanding balance and determine partially_paid vs paid status.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the partner or billing manager who approved this invoice for issuance during the prebilling review process. Required for audit trail and partner sign-off compliance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing partner or authorised approver signed off on the invoice during the prebilling review stage. Marks the transition from draft/WIP to issued status.']
  billing_office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the firm office responsible for billing this invoice. Used for office-level revenue reporting, GL allocation, and multi-office financial consolidation.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Together with billing_period_start, defines the WIP window captured on this bill.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Defines the range of time entries, disbursements, and WIP included in the bill.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the billing system (Elite 3E or Aderant Expert). Provides the audit trail creation marker for the Silver layer record.']
  credit_application_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether a credit note has been applied against outstanding invoices. Pending means unapplied; applied means fully offset; partially_applied means partially offset; voided means the credit was cancelled. Populated only for credit notes.']
  credit_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the issuance of a credit note, including the business justification (e.g., billing error, fee reduction, client dispute resolution, write-off). Populated only when invoice_type = credit_note.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency billing for international clients and cross-border matters.']
  disbursements_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of all client disbursements (hard costs and soft costs) billed on this invoice, including court filing fees, expert witness fees, travel, and photocopying charges.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary discount applied to this invoice, including courtesy discounts, volume discounts, and write-downs negotiated under AFA or client billing guidelines. Reduces the gross fees amount.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Client-provided reason for disputing the invoice (e.g., rate discrepancy, unapproved timekeeper, excessive hours, duplicate charge). Populated when invoice_status = disputed.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which a disputed invoice was resolved, either through client agreement, write-down, or credit note issuance. Used to measure dispute cycle time and collections effectiveness.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is contractually due per the engagement letter or client billing agreement. Used to calculate overdue status and trigger collections workflows.']
  ebilling_portal varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the client\'s e-billing portal to which the LEDES invoice was submitted (e.g., TyMetrix 360, Legal Tracker, BillingPoint, Passport). Null if not submitted electronically.']
  ebilling_rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or narrative returned by the client\'s e-billing portal when an invoice submission is rejected (e.g., invalid UTBMS task code, rate not approved, timekeeper not enrolled). Populated only when ebilling_submission_status = rejected.']
  ebilling_submission_date date [note: 'Date the LEDES-formatted invoice was submitted to the client\'s e-billing portal. Used to track submission timeliness and SLA compliance with client billing guidelines.']
  ebilling_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the LEDES e-billing portal submission. Tracks the full submission lifecycle from initial upload through acceptance or rejection by the client\'s billing system.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Type of fee arrangement governing this invoice. Determines how fees are calculated and reported. AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement) includes fixed, capped, and success-fee structures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|flat_fee|contingency|blended_rate|afa|retainer|success_fee|hybrid — promote to reference product]']
  fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross total of all professional fees (time charges) billed on this invoice before discounts, taxes, or write-downs. Represents the sum of all timekeeper hours multiplied by applicable rates.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code to which this invoice\'s revenue is posted in the firm\'s financial system. Supports SOX-compliant financial reporting and revenue recognition.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The official date the invoice was issued to the client. This is the principal business event date used for revenue recognition, aging calculations, and LEDES e-billing submission headers.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable invoice reference number assigned by the billing system (e.g., Elite 3E or Aderant Expert). Used on all client-facing documents, LEDES e-billing submissions, and payment remittances.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the invoice. Drives AR aging, collections workflow, and revenue recognition. Valid states: draft (prebill stage), issued (sent to client), disputed (client raised objection), paid (fully settled), partially_paid (partial remittance received), written_off (uncollectable balance removed), credited (credit note applied). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|disputed|paid|partially_paid|written_off|credited — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document. Standard is a regular bill; credit_note is a negative invoice reversing or adjusting a prior bill; proforma is a preliminary estimate; interim is a progress bill on a long-running matter; final closes out all WIP on a matter.']
  is_electronic_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is subject to electronic billing via a LEDES-compliant e-billing portal. True = invoice must be submitted electronically; False = paper or email delivery.']
  ledes_format_version varchar [note: 'Version of the LEDES (Legal Electronic Data Exchange Standard) format used for electronic billing submission to corporate clients or insurance carriers. Null if invoice is not submitted via e-billing portal.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount due from the client after applying fees, disbursements, discounts, and taxes. This is the amount the client is expected to remit: (fees_amount + disbursements_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount).']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on this invoice (net_amount minus amount_paid). Drives AR aging buckets and collections prioritisation. Updated upon each payment application.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms applicable to this invoice as defined in the letter of engagement (LOE) or client billing agreement (e.g., net_30 = payment due within 30 days of invoice date).']
  resubmission_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this invoice has been resubmitted to the e-billing portal following a rejection. Tracks resubmission history for operational efficiency and client relationship management.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on this invoice (e.g., VAT, GST, or applicable sales tax). Calculated based on the taxable fee and disbursement amounts and the applicable tax rate for the jurisdiction.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO country or state/province code identifying the tax jurisdiction governing the tax calculation on this invoice. Determines applicable VAT, GST, or sales tax rules.']
  tax_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (as a decimal fraction, e.g., 0.2000 for 20% VAT) used to calculate the tax_amount on this invoice. Varies by jurisdiction and service type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the invoice record in the billing system. Tracks status changes, payment applications, dispute updates, and write-off entries.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount written off on this invoice as uncollectable or as a billing adjustment. Impacts WIP write-off reporting and partner profitability metrics (PPP, PEP).']
  write_off_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative or coded reason for the write-off (e.g., client insolvency, fee dispute settlement, courtesy adjustment, billing error). Required for financial audit trail and partner approval workflows.']

  Note: 'Authoritative billing document issued to a client for legal services rendered on one or more matters. Captures invoice number, invoice date, billing period, total fees, total disbursements, tax amounts, discounts, net amount due, currency, and invoice status lifecycle (draft, issued, disputed, paid, partially paid, written off, credited). Supports LEDES 1998B and LEDES XML electronic billing formats for corporate clients and insurance carriers. Encompasses credit notes (negative invoices with credit reason, originating invoice reference, and application status) and LEDES e-billing portal submission tracking (submission date, format version, target portal, submission status, rejection reasons, and resubmission history). Provides complete invoice-to-payment lifecycle visibility including dispute resolution and collections integration.'
}

Table billing.invoice_line {
  invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each invoice line item within the billing domain. Primary key for the invoice_line data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.asset. Business justification: Invoice line items in IP matters routinely specify which patent, trademark, or copyright the work/expense relates to for client billing guideline compliance, LEDES submission requirements, and portfol']
  billing_disbursement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating disbursement or expense record from which this invoice line was generated. Null for time-based or flat fee lines. Enables disbursement traceability and client cost recovery reconciliation.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Invoice line items reference dockets for detailed billing transparency, LEDES format compliance, and client billing guideline validation in litigation matters.']
  from_time_entry_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.time_entry.time_entry_id — Invoice lines that represent billed time must trace back to their originating time entry for audit trail, LEDES compliance, and dispute resolution. This is the core traceability link in legal billing.']
  hearing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.hearing. Business justification: Line items for hearing-related work (preparation, appearance, follow-up) reference specific hearings for client transparency, UTBMS compliance, and billing guideline validation.']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Invoice line items reference supporting documents (engagement letters for rate justification, court orders for fee awards). Required for LEDES billing compliance, billing guideline validation, and dis']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.legal_service. Business justification: Invoice lines represent individual billable items for a specific legal service. Legal billing systems report revenue by service type at the line level for LEDES compliance, client billing transparency']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter or case to which this billing line item is attributed. Enables matter-level revenue reporting, WIP reconciliation, and client billing guideline compliance checks.']
  primary_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent invoice header to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header/detail relationship for invoice line aggregation and LEDES electronic billing submissions.']
  primary_invoice_time_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating time entry record from which this invoice line was generated. Provides full traceability from the billed line back to the WIP time entry in Elite 3E. Null for disbursement or flat fee lines.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the billing partner, billing coordinator, or supervisor who approved this line item for inclusion on the invoice during the prebilling review process. Supports billing approval workflow audit trails.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this line item was approved during the prebilling review workflow. Used for billing cycle time analysis and audit trail compliance.']
  billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross amount billed to the client for this line item after applying the billing rate to the quantity, and before any write-up or write-down adjustments. Expressed in the invoice currency. Core monetary fact for revenue recognition and AR reporting.']
  billing_guideline_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item has been validated as compliant with the client\'s outside counsel billing guidelines (OCGs). False indicates a potential guideline violation (e.g., block billing, excessive hours, non-compliant timekeeper rate) that may result in client rejection or reduction.']
  billing_guideline_exception_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason this line item was flagged as non-compliant with client billing guidelines. Populated when billing_guideline_compliant is False. Used by billing coordinators to resolve exceptions before invoice submission.']
  billing_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The hourly billing rate applied to this line item in the invoice currency, as agreed in the engagement letter or client rate schedule. Confidential as it reflects negotiated client-specific pricing. Null for disbursement and flat fee lines.']
  client_rejected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was rejected by the client or their legal spend management system after invoice submission. Rejected lines require resolution (write-off, resubmission, or dispute) and are excluded from collected revenue until resolved.']
  client_rejection_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided by the client or their e-billing platform for rejecting this line item. Populated when client_rejected is True. Used to identify systemic billing guideline compliance issues and improve future billing quality.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center or profit center code associated with the practice group or office responsible for this line item. Used for internal profitability reporting, partner compensation calculations, and PPP/PEP financial KPI attribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this invoice line record was first created in the billing system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and billing cycle time analysis in the Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which this line item is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency billing for international matters and foreign currency realization reporting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert this line item from the billing currency to the firm\'s functional currency at the time of invoicing. Relevant for international matters billed in a currency other than the firm\'s base currency. Null for lines in the functional currency.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The type of fee arrangement under which this line item is billed. Reflects the engagement-level fee structure: hourly (standard time-based), contingency (success-based), flat_fee (fixed price), Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA), blended_rate (single rate across timekeepers), or capped_fee (hourly with a ceiling). Drives billing logic and revenue recognition treatment.']
  from_disbursement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.disbursement.disbursement_id — Invoice lines representing expenses must trace back to the originating disbursement record. Required for LEDES compliance and client billing guideline validation.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger (GL) account code to which the revenue or expense for this line item is posted in the firm\'s financial system. Enables reconciliation between the billing sub-ledger and the GL for financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of hours recorded for this time-based line item, expressed in decimal format (e.g., 1.50 for one hour thirty minutes). Null for disbursement and flat fee lines. The billable unit of measure for time charges before rate application.']
  ledes_export_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this line item was last exported in a LEDES electronic billing file for submission to the client or their e-billing platform. Null if the line has not yet been exported. Used to track LEDES submission status and prevent duplicate submissions.']
  ledes_line_item_number varchar [note: 'The line item number as formatted and exported in the LEDES electronic billing file submitted to the client or their legal spend management platform. Ensures traceability between the internal billing record and the external LEDES submission.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this line item within the parent invoice. Used to maintain the presentation order of line items on the invoice as rendered to the client and in LEDES output files.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the invoice line item within the billing lifecycle. Draft lines are in prebilling review; approved lines are ready for invoice generation; billed lines have been sent to the client; written_off lines have been removed from AR; on_hold lines are pending client or internal review.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice line item indicating whether it represents a time charge, a disbursement/expense, a flat fee component, an Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) component, a tax charge, or a credit adjustment. Drives downstream billing logic and LEDES field mapping.']
  lpp_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the narrative or details of this line item are subject to Legal Professional Privilege (LPP) and must be redacted or withheld in any disclosure to third parties. Critical for eDiscovery, regulatory investigations, and client data sharing compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this invoice line record was last modified in the billing system. Tracks prebilling edits, write-up/write-down adjustments, and status changes for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'The descriptive text narrative for this line item as it appears on the client invoice. Describes the legal work performed or disbursement incurred. Subject to billing guideline requirements for narrative specificity. May be edited from the original time entry description during prebilling review.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount for this line item after applying write-up/write-down adjustments to the billed amount. This is the amount that contributes to the invoice total and is recognized as revenue. Equals billed_amount plus write_up_down_amount.']
  original_narrative varchar [note: 'The unedited narrative text from the originating time entry or disbursement record before any prebilling edits were applied. Retained for audit trail purposes and to support billing guideline compliance reviews comparing original versus billed descriptions.']
  phase_code varchar [note: 'The matter phase or stage code to which this line item is attributed (e.g., Phase 1 - Due Diligence, Phase 2 - Negotiation). Used in phased billing arrangements and Legal Project Management (LPM) budget tracking to allocate costs to defined matter phases.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity or unit count for this line item. For time lines this mirrors hours_worked; for disbursement lines it represents the number of units (e.g., pages copied, miles travelled). Used in conjunction with billing_rate to derive the billed amount.']
  sources_disbursement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.disbursement.disbursement_id — Invoice lines that represent billed disbursements must trace back to the originating disbursement record for cost recovery tracking and LEDES compliance.']
  standard_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The timekeeper\'s standard rack rate at the time of billing, before any client-specific discounts or negotiated rate reductions. Used to calculate write-up/write-down variance and to support rate realization analysis for PPP and RPE KPI reporting.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount applicable to this line item (e.g., VAT, GST, or sales tax) calculated based on the applicable tax code and jurisdiction. Null if the line item is tax-exempt. Required for tax reporting and compliance with jurisdiction-specific tax regulations.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to this line item identifying the applicable tax rate and jurisdiction rule (e.g., VAT20, EXEMPT, ZERO). Drives automated tax calculation in Elite 3E and supports VAT/GST return preparation and audit compliance.']
  timekeeper_level varchar [note: 'The seniority classification of the timekeeper who performed the work on this line item (e.g., partner, senior associate, associate, paralegal). Denormalized from the timekeeper record for billing analytics and client billing guideline compliance without requiring a join. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: partner|senior_associate|associate|paralegal|trainee|of_counsel|staff_attorney|law_clerk — promote to reference product]']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS activity code describing the specific activity performed within the task (e.g., A101 for Plan and Prepare, A102 for Research). Used alongside the task code in LEDES billing files to provide granular task-based cost management data to clients.']
  utbms_expense_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS expense code classifying the type of disbursement on this line item (e.g., E101 for Copying, E106 for Computer Research). Populated only for disbursement line types. Required for LEDES electronic billing of expense items.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'UTBMS task code classifying the legal work performed on this line item (e.g., L110 for Fact Investigation, L210 for Pleadings). Mandatory for LEDES electronic billing submissions and client billing guideline compliance. Sourced from the UTBMS code set maintained by the ABA and ACCA.']
  wip_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original Work in Progress (WIP) value of this line item at the standard billing rate before any prebilling adjustments, write-ups, or write-downs. Used to calculate the realization rate (net_amount / wip_amount) and to reconcile WIP balances in the general ledger.']
  work_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the legal work or disbursement represented by this line item was performed. Distinct from the invoice date. Used for WIP aging analysis, billing guideline compliance (e.g., time entry submission within N days of work), and matter timeline reporting.']
  write_off_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for any write-off or write-down applied to this line item (e.g., CLIENT_DISPUTE, BILLING_ERROR, COURTESY_REDUCTION, BUDGET_CAP). Populated when write_up_down_amount is negative. Supports write-off analysis and partner compensation adjustments.']
  write_up_down_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary adjustment applied to this line item at the prebilling or billing stage. A positive value represents a write-up (increase above standard); a negative value represents a write-down (reduction below standard). Critical for realization rate analysis and partner compensation calculations.']

  Note: 'Individual line item on a legal invoice representing a single time entry, disbursement, or flat fee component. Captures UTBMS task code, activity code, timekeeper, narrative, quantity, rate, amount, tax code, and line-level write-up/write-down adjustments. Provides the granular detail required for LEDES electronic billing submissions and client billing guideline compliance. Each line traces back to an originating time entry or disbursement record.'
}

Table billing.fee_arrangement {
  fee_arrangement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the fee arrangement record. Primary key for the fee_arrangement data product in the billing domain.']
  delivery_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.delivery_model. Business justification: The delivery model (managed service, project-based, on-demand) directly determines fee arrangement structure and AFA type. Legal pricing teams structure fee arrangements based on delivery model. This ']
  engagement_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.engagement_opportunity. Business justification: Engagement opportunities capture AFA type and estimated matter value that directly feed fee arrangement setup. Business development reporting on won-opportunity-to-fee-arrangement conversion and AFA a']
  guideline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_guideline. Business justification: Fee arrangements must comply with client-specific billing guidelines (outside counsel guidelines) that govern rate caps, timekeeper restrictions, and billing format requirements. Currently fee_arrange']
  legal_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.legal_service. Business justification: Fee arrangements govern how a specific legal service is billed. Legal pricing teams negotiate AFAs and fixed fees for defined service scopes. billing_fee_arrangement links to pricing_model and rate_ca']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the matter or case to which this fee arrangement applies. A fee arrangement governs how WIP is converted to invoiced amounts for the associated matter.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.package. Business justification: Fee arrangements are frequently structured around a service package (e.g., fixed-fee litigation package, M&A transaction package). Legal operations teams track which service package a fee arrangement ']
  parent_arrangement_billing_fee_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original or predecessor fee arrangement that this record amends or supersedes. Enables reconstruction of the full amendment history chain. Null for original (version 1) arrangements.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Fee arrangements are scoped to practice areas for engagement structuring, conflict checking, and approval workflows. Practice area determines pricing authority, AFA eligibility, and engagement letter ']
  pricing_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.pricing_model. Business justification: Fee arrangements implement pricing models (hourly, fixed fee, contingency, blended, AFA). Pricing model defines calculation rules, billing frequency, payment terms, and revenue recognition used in eng']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity that is party to this fee arrangement. Supports portfolio-level fee arrangements spanning multiple matters for the same client.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the negotiated rate card defining timekeeper-level or role-level hourly rates applicable under this fee arrangement. Used for hourly and blended rate arrangements to constrain rates during time entry and prebilling in Aderant Expert.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Fee arrangements must comply with specific regulatory obligations (SRA transparency rules, consumer protection pricing disclosure). Legal compliance teams track which regulatory obligation mandates ea']
  rfp_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.rfp_submission. Business justification: Won RFP submissions specify fee arrangement types and estimated values that directly govern fee arrangement creation. Pitch-to-revenue reporting and panel compliance tracking require tracing fee arran']
  sla_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_template. Business justification: Fee arrangements in legal firms incorporate SLA commitments (response time guarantees, delivery milestones tied to fee structure). Legal operations teams link SLA templates to fee arrangements for bre']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.tier. Business justification: Client service tier directly determines fee arrangement terms and AFA eligibility (Tier 1 clients receive different structures). Legal pricing teams set fee arrangements based on client tier. This lin']
  agreed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total agreed fee amount for flat fee, capped fee, retainer, subscription, and success fee arrangements. Represents the contractually committed monetary value. For hourly and blended rate arrangements, this field is null and rate fields govern billing. Expressed in the arrangement currency.']
  amendment_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number tracking amendments to the fee arrangement. Version 1 represents the original arrangement; subsequent amendments increment this counter. Supports audit trail and supersession chain tracking in Elite 3E.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the partner or authorized approver who sanctioned this fee arrangement on behalf of the firm. Required for AFA arrangements above firm-defined approval thresholds. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee arrangement was formally approved by the authorized partner or pricing committee. Marks the transition from draft to active status and triggers engagement setup workflows in Elite 3E.']
  arrangement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the fee arrangement, used in client communications, engagement letters (LOE), and billing correspondence. Typically follows firm-defined numbering conventions in Elite 3E.']
  arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fee arrangement. Controls whether the arrangement is actively governing billing operations. Draft indicates pending approval; active indicates currently in force; suspended indicates temporarily paused; expired indicates past effective end date; terminated indicates early cancellation; superseded indicates replaced by a newer arrangement.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Agreed cadence at which invoices are generated and submitted to the client under this fee arrangement. Governs the prebilling cycle in Elite 3E and Aderant Expert. Milestone-based frequency requires milestone trigger configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|milestone|on_demand|upon_completion — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  blended_hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Single agreed hourly rate applied uniformly across all timekeepers on the matter, regardless of individual timekeeper seniority or standard rate. Used for blended rate AFA arrangements. Null for non-blended arrangements.']
  contingency_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the recovery or settlement amount payable to the firm under a contingency fee arrangement. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 33.3333 for 33.33%). Null for non-contingency arrangement types. Governed by jurisdiction-specific caps on contingency fees.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee arrangement record was first created in the system. Represents the audit creation event. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per firm data standards.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the fee arrangement amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Governs currency conversion rules during invoice generation and financial reporting.']
  discount_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Negotiated discount percentage applied to standard rates or fees under this arrangement. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 10.0000 for 10%). Reduces the billable amount during invoice generation. Supports client relationship management and competitive pricing strategies.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the fee arrangement becomes binding and governs billing for the associated matter or client portfolio. Aligns with the engagement commencement date in the Letter of Engagement (LOE).']
  expense_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum disbursement or expense amount the firm may incur and bill to the client under this arrangement without prior written approval. Governs disbursement billing controls during invoice generation.']
  expenses_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether disbursements and out-of-pocket expenses are billable to the client under this fee arrangement. When false, all expenses are absorbed by the firm. Governs disbursement line inclusion during prebilling.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee arrangement ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended arrangements. When populated, triggers review or renewal workflows in Elite 3E. Distinct from termination date which reflects early cancellation.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum billable amount under a capped fee or blended rate arrangement. WIP accrued beyond this threshold cannot be invoiced without client approval or a formal cap amendment. Enforced during prebilling in Elite 3E and Aderant Expert.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country code identifying the primary legal jurisdiction governing this fee arrangement. Determines applicable professional conduct rules, fee cap regulations, tax treatment, and LEDES billing format requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the fee arrangement record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per firm data standards.']
  ledes_billing_format varchar [note: 'LEDES electronic billing format required by the client for invoice submission. Governs the output format of invoices generated from this arrangement. Clients with e-billing portals (e.g., TyMetrix, Legal Tracker) mandate specific LEDES versions.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which the client is contractually required to remit payment. Standard terms include net-30, net-45, net-60. Used to calculate invoice due dates and trigger collections workflows in Elite 3E accounts receivable.']
  portfolio_arrangement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee arrangement governs a portfolio of multiple matters for the same client rather than a single matter. Portfolio arrangements aggregate WIP and billing across matters and require consolidated invoice generation.']
  retainer_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Periodic retainer fee amount payable by the client under a retainer or subscription arrangement. Represents the recurring fixed payment that secures the firm\'s availability. Tracked against IOLTA trust account rules where applicable.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the legal services scope covered by this fee arrangement. Defines the boundaries of work included within the agreed fee, distinguishing in-scope from out-of-scope services that would require a separate arrangement or amendment.']
  success_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount payable as a success fee upon achievement of the defined trigger condition. Distinct from contingency_rate_pct which is percentage-based. Null for non-success-fee arrangements.']
  success_fee_trigger varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the milestone, outcome, or event that triggers the success fee component of the arrangement (e.g., deal closing, favourable judgment, regulatory approval). Applicable only to success fee and hybrid AFA arrangements.']
  tax_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether applicable taxes (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax) are chargeable on invoices generated under this fee arrangement. Drives tax calculation logic in Elite 3E and Aderant Expert during invoice generation.']
  tax_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage (e.g., VAT, GST) applied to invoices generated under this arrangement when tax_applicable is true. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 20.0000 for 20% UK VAT). Null when tax_applicable is false.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee arrangement was early-terminated prior to its scheduled expiry date. Populated only when arrangement_status is terminated. Distinct from expiry_date which reflects the originally agreed end date.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the fee arrangement was terminated prior to its scheduled expiry. Supports client relationship analytics and risk management reporting. Populated only when arrangement_status is terminated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: matter_closed|client_request|firm_withdrawal|superseded|breach|regulatory|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  utbms_task_code_set varchar [note: 'UTBMS task code set applicable to this fee arrangement, governing how time entries and disbursements are categorized for task-based billing. Required for LEDES-compliant invoice submission and client billing guideline compliance.']
  wip_conversion_rule varchar [note: 'Rule governing how accumulated WIP is converted to invoiced amounts under this fee arrangement. Determines the timing and method of WIP recognition during prebilling. Critical for revenue recognition and financial reporting under the billing domain.']
  write_off_limit_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Maximum percentage of WIP that the billing attorney may write off during prebilling without escalating to partner or management approval. Enforces write-off governance controls in Aderant Expert and Elite 3E.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the agreed fee structure between the firm and a client for a specific matter or portfolio of matters.
