Captures AFA type (hourly, flat fee, contingency, capped fee, blended rate, success fee, retainer, subscription), agreed amounts or rate caps, billing frequency, milestone triggers, and approval metadata. Governs how WIP is converted to invoiced amounts and constrains the billing rules applied during prebilling and invoice generation.'
}

Table billing.billing_payment {
  billing_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each payment transaction record in the billing system. Primary key for the payment data product.']
  aml_kyc_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_kyc_program. Business justification: Legal firms must screen payments under a specific AML/KYC program (FATF, SRA requirements). Tracing which AML program governed a payment\'s screening is a named compliance process; aml_screening_status']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client trust or IOLTA account from which funds were transferred, applicable when payment_type is \'trust_transfer\' or \'retainer_drawdown\'. Supports IOLTA compliance, trust accounting reconciliation, and regulatory reporting.']
  billing_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the firm\'s internal bank account record into which the payment was deposited (e.g., operating account, IOLTA/trust account, client account). Supports bank reconciliation and IOLTA compliance reporting.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Clients often remit payments referencing specific dockets/cases. Tracking payment-to-docket enables matter-level AR reconciliation, client account management, and litigation cost recovery tracking.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary invoice satisfied by this payment. For partial or multi-invoice payments, the dominant invoice is recorded here; full allocation detail is captured in the payment allocation lines.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary matter against which this payment is applied. For multi-matter payments, the dominant or first-allocated matter is recorded here; full allocation detail is captured in the payment allocation lines.']
  original_payment_billing_payment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original payment record when this record represents a reversal, reapplication, or correction of a prior payment. Enables payment lineage tracing and audit trail for reversed and reprocessed payments.']
  paying_organisation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.organisation. Business justification: Corporate treasury departments often make payments on behalf of multiple subsidiaries/client_profiles. Tracking the paying organisation (which may differ from the client_profile\'s organisation) enable']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client who remitted the payment. Links the payment to the client master record for accounts receivable aging and collections tracking.']
  retainer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retainer agreement or retainer balance record from which funds are drawn, applicable when payment_type is \'retainer_drawdown\'. Links the payment to the pre-paid retainer for balance tracking and client reporting.']
  trust_ledger_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.ledger_entry. Business justification: Billing payments drawn from trust must be traceable to the corresponding trust ledger entry recording the disbursement. This trust-to-office transfer reconciliation is a mandatory legal trust accounti']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Describes how the received funds are distributed across outstanding invoices. \'full\' means the entire invoice balance is satisfied; \'partial\' means only a portion of one or more invoices is covered; \'retainer_drawdown\' means funds are drawn from a pre-paid retainer; \'trust_transfer\' means funds originate from a client trust account; \'unapplied\' means no allocation has been made yet.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of the received funds that has been allocated and posted against one or more outstanding invoices. For fully applied payments, equals received_amount. For partially applied payments, is less than received_amount.']
  applied_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the received funds were allocated and posted against outstanding invoices in the billing system. May differ from received_date when payments are held in unapplied status pending client direction or prebilling approval.']
  bank_reference varchar [note: 'The unique transaction reference number assigned by the receiving bank or payment processor for this payment (e.g., SWIFT UETR, CHAPS reference, ACH trace number, cheque number). Used for bank reconciliation, payment tracing, and AML (Anti-Money Laundering) compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the payment record was first created in the billing system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with record-keeping obligations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the currency in which the payment was received from the client (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency billing and foreign exchange reconciliation.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any prompt-payment or early-settlement discount granted to the client and applied at the time of payment, reducing the net amount required to satisfy the invoice. Distinct from write_off_amount which represents a negotiated fee reduction.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the received_amount from the payment currency to the firm\'s functional (base) currency at the time of receipt. A value of 1.0 indicates the payment was received in the firm\'s functional currency. Required for multi-currency AR reporting and financial consolidation.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The type of fee arrangement under which the invoices satisfied by this payment were billed. \'hourly\' is standard time-based billing; \'flat_fee\' is a fixed amount per matter or task; \'contingency\' is a percentage of recovery; \'afa\' is an Alternative Fee Arrangement (AFA) such as capped fees or success fees; \'blended_rate\' is a single rate across all timekeepers; \'retainer\' is a pre-paid retainer arrangement.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The received_amount converted to the firm\'s functional (base) currency using the exchange_rate at the time of receipt. Used for consolidated financial reporting, PPP (Profit Per Partner), PEP (Profit Per Equity Partner), and RPE (Revenue Per Equity Partner) KPI calculations.']
  gl_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (YYYY-MM) to which this payment is posted in the General Ledger (GL). Used for period-close financial reporting, revenue recognition, and SOX compliance. May differ from the calendar month of received_date due to period-end adjustments.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was posted to the firm\'s General Ledger (GL), which may differ from the received_date or applied_date due to period-end cut-off rules. Critical for financial period reporting, SOX compliance, and revenue recognition.']
  is_multi_matter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment has been allocated across two or more matters. When True, the full allocation detail is captured in the payment allocation lines product. Supports complex multi-matter payment scenario reporting and client account management.']
  is_reversed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment has been reversed (e.g., due to a returned cheque, failed ACH, or client dispute). When True, the reversal_date and reversal_reason fields are populated and the previously applied invoice balances are reinstated.']
  is_trust_payment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment involves a transfer from a client trust or IOLTA account to the firm\'s operating account. When True, the payment is subject to IOLTA compliance rules and trust accounting reconciliation requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for billing staff to record additional context about the payment, such as client instructions, special allocation directions, collection negotiation notes, or explanations for partial payments. Not intended for structured data.']
  overpayment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount by which the received funds exceed the total outstanding balance of the invoices to which the payment was applied. A non-zero value requires a decision to refund, credit, or hold as a retainer. Supports client credit and refund workflows.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The instrument or mechanism used by the client to remit funds. \'wire_transfer\' covers SWIFT/CHAPS/SEPA bank wires; \'cheque\' covers paper cheques; \'ach\' covers ACH/BACS electronic transfers; \'credit_card\' covers card payments; \'trust_transfer\' covers transfers from the client\'s trust/IOLTA account to the office account; \'retainer_drawdown\' covers application of a pre-paid retainer balance.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the payment, used in client remittance advice, bank reconciliation, and collections correspondence. Follows the firm\'s sequential payment numbering convention (e.g., PAY-2024-000123).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment within the accounts receivable workflow. \'received\' indicates funds have been confirmed in the bank; \'applied\' means fully allocated to invoices; \'partially_applied\' means some funds remain unapplied; \'unapplied\' means no allocation has occurred; \'reversed\' means the payment was reversed after application; \'voided\' means the payment was cancelled before application.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the payment transaction for accounting and AR purposes. \'standard\' is a regular client remittance; \'retainer_drawdown\' is an application of a pre-paid retainer; \'trust_transfer\' is a transfer from a client trust/IOLTA account; \'overpayment_credit\' is a credit applied from a prior overpayment; \'write_off_reversal\' is a recovery of a previously written-off amount.']
  received_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross amount of funds received from the client in the transaction currency, as confirmed by the bank or trust account statement. This is the authoritative amount before any exchange rate conversion or allocation.']
  received_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the firm confirmed receipt of funds from the client, as recorded in the bank or trust account. This is the principal business event date used for accounts receivable aging, cash flow reporting, and revenue recognition.']
  remittance_reference varchar [note: 'The reference or description provided by the client on the remittance advice or payment instruction, typically including invoice numbers, matter references, or client-side purchase order numbers. Used to match incoming funds to outstanding invoices during cash application.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the payment was reversed in the billing system, reinstating the outstanding invoice balances. Populated only when is_reversed is True. Used for AR aging recalculation and collections workflow re-triggering.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the payment was reversed. \'returned_cheque\' indicates a dishonoured cheque; \'failed_ach\' indicates a failed electronic transfer; \'client_dispute\' indicates the client contested the payment; \'duplicate_payment\' indicates the payment was made twice; \'bank_error\' indicates a banking error; \'other\' covers miscellaneous reasons. Populated only when is_reversed is True.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the received funds that has not yet been allocated to any invoice (received_amount minus applied_amount). A non-zero value triggers follow-up in the collections and AR workflow. Supports unapplied balance reporting and client credit management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the payment record, including status changes, allocation updates, or reversal processing. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  wip_cleared_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of Work in Progress (WIP) that was cleared from the WIP ledger as a result of this payment being applied. Relevant for contingency and AFA matters where WIP is recognized upon collection rather than billing. Supports WIP management and revenue recognition reporting.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of the outstanding invoice balance that was written off in conjunction with this payment, representing the difference between the invoiced amount and the amount the client agreed to pay (e.g., in a negotiated settlement or fee reduction). Supports bad debt reporting and partner write-off approval workflows.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of funds received from a client against one or more invoices, including the detailed allocation of received funds across outstanding invoices and line items. Captures payment date, payment method (wire transfer, cheque, ACH, credit card), amount received, currency, exchange rate, bank reference, remittance details, and per-invoice/per-line allocation amounts with allocation type (full, partial, retainer drawdown, trust transfer). Supports partial payments, overpayments, unapplied balances, retainer drawdowns, trust-to-office account transfers, and complex multi-matter payment scenarios. Feeds accounts receivable aging and collections workflows.'
}

Table billing.payment_allocation {
  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each payment allocation record. Primary key for the payment_allocation data product in the billing domain.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IOLTA or client trust account from which funds were drawn for this allocation, when allocation_type is \'trust_transfer\'. Required for trust accounting compliance and state bar regulatory reporting.']
  billing_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent payment record from which funds are being allocated. Links this allocation to the originating payment transaction.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Payment allocations are applied to invoices for specific dockets. Required for matter-level AR accuracy, client account reconciliation, and litigation profitability tracking.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice against which this allocation is applied. Supports multi-invoice payment scenarios common in large corporate client matters.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice line item against which this allocation is applied. Enables granular line-level allocation tracking for task-based billing under UTBMS standards.']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter associated with this payment allocation. Supports multi-matter payment scenarios where a single client payment is distributed across multiple active matters.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity making the payment. Enables client-level AR aging analysis and supports consolidated billing for clients with multiple matters.']
  retainer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retainer account from which funds were drawn when allocation_type is \'retainer_drawdown\'. Tracks the drawdown of pre-paid retainer balances against earned fees.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount from the payment being applied to the referenced invoice or invoice line. Core financial field for AR aging, cash application, and revenue recognition. Must be positive and not exceed the payment\'s unapplied balance.']
  allocated_amount_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The allocated amount expressed in the firm\'s functional (base) currency after applying the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting, PPP, PEP, and RPE KPI calculations.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The business date on which the payment allocation was applied to the invoice. Determines the AR aging bucket reduction and is the principal event date for cash application reporting and period-end close.']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for this payment allocation record, used in client communications, remittance advice reconciliation, and audit trails. Typically follows firm-defined numbering conventions in Elite 3E or Aderant Expert.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment allocation record. \'posted\' indicates funds have been applied to the invoice; \'reversed\' indicates a corrective reversal has been processed; \'pending\' indicates awaiting approval or trust transfer confirmation; \'voided\' indicates the allocation was cancelled; \'unapplied\' indicates funds received but not yet matched to an invoice.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the payment is being applied. \'full\' covers the entire outstanding balance; \'partial\' covers a portion; \'retainer_drawdown\' applies funds held in a retainer account; \'trust_transfer\' applies funds from an IOLTA trust account; \'write_off_offset\' offsets a previously written-off amount; \'credit_memo\' applies a credit note against the invoice balance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for this payment allocation, particularly relevant for trust transfers and write-off offsets that require partner or billing manager sign-off per firm policy.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the billing manager, partner, or authorized approver who approved this payment allocation. Required for audit trail and compliance with firm billing governance policies.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the payment allocation was approved by the authorized approver. Part of the allocation lifecycle audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this payment allocation record was first created in the system. Part of the mandatory audit trail for financial records.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the allocated amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency billing in international legal matters, particularly relevant for ICC and LCIA arbitration matters.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any prompt-payment or negotiated discount applied at the time of payment allocation, reducing the effective amount required to satisfy the invoice balance. Tracked separately from write-offs for financial reporting purposes.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the allocated amount from the payment currency to the firm\'s functional currency at the time of allocation. Relevant for cross-border matters billed in non-functional currencies.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The billing fee arrangement type governing the invoice to which this allocation is applied. Supports analysis of cash collection performance across different fee structures including AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement), contingency, and hourly billing.']
  gl_post_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation was posted to the General Ledger. May differ from allocation_date due to period-end cutoff rules or delayed GL posting. Critical for financial period reconciliation and SOX compliance.']
  invoice_balance_after decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining outstanding invoice balance immediately after this allocation is applied. Enables real-time AR aging bucket calculation and identifies fully satisfied versus partially paid invoices.']
  invoice_balance_before decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding invoice balance immediately prior to this allocation being applied. Provides a point-in-time snapshot for audit trail purposes and supports AR aging reconciliation.']
  is_auto_allocated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation was applied automatically by the billing system (True) or manually by a billing administrator (False). Supports audit trail requirements and cash application workflow quality monitoring.']
  is_trust_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation has been reviewed and confirmed as compliant with applicable trust accounting rules (True) or flagged for review (False). Required for IOLTA compliance and state bar regulatory reporting.']
  ledes_billing_period varchar [note: 'The billing period (YYYY-MM) associated with the invoice to which this allocation is applied, formatted per LEDES standards. Supports electronic billing reconciliation with corporate clients and insurance carriers.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this payment allocation record was last updated. Supports change data capture, audit trail requirements, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by billing staff regarding this payment allocation, such as client instructions, special payment conditions, or dispute context. Supports collections workflow and client communication documentation.']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the firm office or practice group location associated with the matter for which this allocation is applied. Supports office-level revenue and collections performance reporting.']
  remittance_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number or description provided by the client on their remittance advice, used to match the payment to the correct invoice(s). Critical for automated cash application and reconciliation in LEDES-compliant billing workflows.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for reversing this payment allocation (e.g., returned check, incorrect invoice, client dispute). Populated only when allocation_status is \'reversed\'. Supports collections and dispute resolution workflows.']
  reversed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this payment allocation was reversed. Populated only when allocation_status is \'reversed\'. Used for AR aging correction and period-end reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this payment allocation record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Elite 3E and Aderant Expert in hybrid or transitioning environments.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this payment allocation record in the originating source system (e.g., Elite 3E transaction ID or Aderant Expert allocation key). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  unapplied_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the payment that remains unallocated after this allocation record is processed. A non-zero value indicates funds on account that require further application to outstanding invoices or return to the client.']
  value_date date [note: 'The date on which the funds were considered cleared and available, as confirmed by the bank or payment processor. Used for trust accounting reconciliation and IOLTA compliance reporting.']
  wip_cleared_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of WIP (Work in Progress) cleared from the matter\'s WIP ledger as a result of this payment allocation. Relevant for revenue recognition and matter profitability reporting.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount written off in conjunction with this payment allocation, representing the difference between the invoice balance and the payment received when a partial settlement is accepted. Impacts PPP and PEP calculations.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a payment to one or more invoice lines, capturing how received funds are distributed across outstanding invoices and line items. Tracks allocated amount, allocation date, allocation type (full, partial, retainer drawdown, trust transfer), and any unapplied balance. Ensures accurate AR aging and supports complex multi-matter payment scenarios common in large corporate client relationships.'
}

Table billing.ar_balance {
  ar_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accounts receivable balance record in the AR aging ledger. Primary key for the ar_balance data product.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_agreement. Business justification: AR collections in legal services require direct reference to the governing contract agreement for credit terms, dispute resolution rights, and payment terms enforcement. Collections teams reference th']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: AR aging is tracked at the docket/matter level for litigation matters. Essential for collections prioritization, client creditworthiness assessment, and litigation portfolio management.']
  matter_risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.matter_risk_profile. Business justification: Client credit risk reporting: AR aging buckets and collection status directly populate client_credit_risk_rating and client_payment_history_flag on matter_risk_profile. Legal finance teams link AR bal']
  organisation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity who owes the outstanding balance. Links the AR record to the client master for collections management and partner financial reviews.']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: AR balances are monitored by practice area for collections prioritization, write-off authority, and cash flow forecasting. Practice area leaders manage collections strategy and approve write-offs with']
  primary_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating invoice that generated this AR balance record. Supports invoice-level aging and dispute tracking.']
  primary_ar_matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific legal matter against which the outstanding balance is recorded. Enables matter-level AR aging and WIP reconciliation.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_profile. Business justification: AR aging reports, credit limit monitoring, and client payment behavior analysis require aggregation at client_profile level across all matters. Currently ar_balance links to organisation and matter bu']
  trust_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trust.account. Business justification: Legal finance AR aging reports must show available trust funds offsetting outstanding AR balances. Linking ar_balance to the client\'s trust account enables the standard legal finance \'AR vs. trust off']
  write_off_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.write_off. Business justification: AR balances may be partially or fully written off when deemed uncollectible. Currently ar_balance has write_off_amount, write_off_date, and write_off_approved_by as denormalized attributes, but no FK ']
  ar_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this AR balance record. Used in client statements, collection correspondence, and LEDES electronic billing submissions.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed billing cycle frequency for this matter as specified in the Letter of Engagement (LOE). Determines invoice generation cadence and expected AR turnover.']
  bucket_0_30_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the outstanding balance aged 0 to 30 days from the invoice date. Used in the standard AR aging report for partner financial reviews and PPP/PEP calculations.']
  bucket_31_60_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the outstanding balance aged 31 to 60 days from the invoice date. Elevated aging bucket that may trigger initial collection outreach per firm collections policy.']
  bucket_61_90_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the outstanding balance aged 61 to 90 days from the invoice date. Triggers escalated collection action and partner notification under standard firm collections protocols.']
  bucket_over_90_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the outstanding balance aged more than 90 days from the invoice date. Highest-risk aging bucket; typically triggers write-off evaluation, external collections referral, or matter suspension.']
  client_matter_number varchar [note: 'Combined client-matter reference code (e.g., 12345.001) as used in Elite 3E and on client invoices. Standard identifier for cross-referencing billing records with matter management systems.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current collections lifecycle state of the AR balance record. Drives workflow routing in Aderant Expert collections management and partner escalation processes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current|overdue|in_dispute|sent_to_collections|written_off|settled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  collections_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the collections team or responsible partner regarding collection activity, client commitments, payment plans, or dispute context for this AR balance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this AR balance record was first created in the system, typically upon invoice posting. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX and data governance compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the invoice and AR balance are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency billing for international matters.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net outstanding amount currently owed by the client on this AR record after applying all payments, credits, and write-offs to date. The authoritative balance figure for collections management and financial reporting.']
  days_outstanding varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed since the invoice date. Used as the primary aging metric for AR bucket assignment and collections prioritisation. Recalculated daily during Silver layer processing.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has formally disputed this invoice or AR balance. When true, collections activity is paused pending resolution and the record is flagged for billing partner review.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Categorised reason for the client\'s invoice dispute. Populated when dispute_flag is true. Supports root-cause analysis of billing disputes and UTBMS task code compliance reviews. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fee_dispute|task_code_error|rate_discrepancy|duplicate_invoice|scope_dispute|other — promote to reference product if additional reasons are required]']
  due_date date [note: 'The contractual payment due date for the invoice as defined by the credit terms in the Letter of Engagement (LOE). Determines overdue status and triggers collection workflows.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the billing currency to the firm\'s functional reporting currency at the time of invoice generation. Required for multi-currency AR consolidation and financial reporting.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The billing fee structure governing this matter\'s invoicing. Determines how WIP is converted to billed amounts and how AR is recognised. Supports AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement), contingency, hourly, flat fee, blended rate, and retainer models.']
  interest_accrued decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual interest amount accrued on the overdue AR balance where the Letter of Engagement (LOE) provides for late payment interest. Tracked separately from the principal balance for financial reporting.']
  iolta_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AR balance is associated with funds held in an IOLTA trust account. When true, payment processing must comply with IOLTA rules and state bar trust accounting regulations.']
  last_collection_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent collections outreach activity (call, email, or letter) made to the client regarding this outstanding balance. Supports collections workflow management and SLA compliance.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the most recent payment applied to this AR balance. Supports cash flow analysis and collections follow-up.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received and applied against this AR balance. Used in collections management to assess client payment behaviour and prioritise outreach.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'Date the most recent client account statement was generated and dispatched for this AR balance. Used to track statement cycle compliance and client communication history.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this AR balance record, including payment applications, write-offs, credit adjustments, or status changes. Required for incremental Silver layer processing and audit compliance.']
  matter_type_code varchar [note: 'Classification code for the type of legal matter (e.g., M&A, Litigation, IP Prosecution, Employment Advisory) associated with this AR balance. Enables matter-type segmentation in AR analytics.']
  original_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross billed amount on the original invoice before any payments, credits, or write-offs are applied. Denominated in the billing currency. Forms the basis for AR aging and revenue recognition.']
  total_credits_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative value of credit notes, billing adjustments, and courtesy discounts applied against this AR balance. Distinct from write-offs; represents negotiated or corrective reductions.']
  total_payments_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of all client payments applied against this AR balance record to date. Reconciles original invoice amount to current outstanding balance.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable balance record representing the outstanding amount owed by a client at the matter and invoice level. Tracks current balance, aged buckets (0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days), last payment date, last statement date, credit terms, and collection status. Serves as the authoritative AR aging ledger for collections management, partner financial reviews, and PPP/PEP reporting.'
}

Table billing.write_off {
  write_off_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each write-off or write-down transaction record in the billing system. Primary key for the write_off data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ip.ip_asset. Business justification: When IP assets are abandoned or lapsed, associated WIP and AR are written off in a named IP portfolio wind-down process. Linking write-offs to the specific IP asset supports portfolio cost reconciliat']
  conflict_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to conflict.check. Business justification: Conflict-driven write-off reporting: when a conflict is discovered post-engagement, fees billed during the conflicted period must be written off. Legal risk and billing teams require the link between ']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Write-offs are authorized at the matter/docket level. Required for litigation profitability analysis, partner approval workflows, and accurate matter-level revenue recognition.']
  indemnity_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.indemnity_claim. Business justification: Write-offs may be linked to PI claims (settlement amounts, defence costs, fee reductions). Real business process: claim financial impact tracking requires linking write-offs to claims for reserve mana']
  indemnity_exposure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.indemnity_exposure. Business justification: PI claim cost tracking: write-offs are frequently triggered by indemnity exposures (firm writes off fees when settling a PI claim). Legal risk teams need to link write-off amounts to indemnity_exposur']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal matter against which the write-off is recorded. Enables matter-level profitability analysis, realization rate calculations, and WIP aging reporting.']
  organisation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.organisation. Business justification: Write-off approval thresholds and bad debt reserves are managed at organisation level for corporate groups. Tracking the organisation enables consolidated write-off reporting, credit risk assessment a']
  pi_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pi_claim. Business justification: PI claim cost quantification for insurance reporting: write-offs directly resulting from PI claim settlements must be linked to the pi_claim record to calculate total claim cost (defence costs + write']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Write-offs require practice area leader approval and impact practice area P&L. Practice area determines approval authority thresholds, write-off policies, and profitability reporting used in partner c']
  primary_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — AR write-offs reference the specific invoice being written off. Essential for AR reconciliation and realization rate calculation.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client entity associated with the write-off. Supports client-level write-off trend analysis, relationship management reporting, and bad debt exposure tracking.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_register. Business justification: Material write-offs (especially pattern write-offs by timekeeper or client) are risk events requiring registration for trend analysis, partner accountability, and realization rate monitoring. Firms es']
  reversed_write_off_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original write-off record that this entry reverses. Populated only when is_reversal is True. Enables audit trail linking between original write-offs and their reversals for GL reconciliation and financial period close.']
  wip_ledger_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.wip_ledger.wip_ledger_id — WIP write-offs must reference the WIP ledger entry being written off. Critical for realization rate calculations and ensuring WIP balances are properly reduced.']
  write_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Bad debt write-offs must reference the invoice being written off from AR. Required for AR balance accuracy and financial close procedures.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value formally written off or written down, expressed in the billing currency. Must be less than or equal to original_amount. This is the primary financial impact figure used in realization rate, profitability, and financial period close reporting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the approving partner formally authorized the write-off. May differ from write_off_date if approval precedes the accounting period posting. Critical for partner approval audit trails and governance compliance.']
  client_agreed_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'The negotiated or agreed billing rate for the timekeeper applicable to this client/matter at the time of the write-off. Enables analysis of rate realization versus standard rates and identification of billing guideline-driven write-offs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this write-off record was first created in the system of record. Represents the audit trail creation event. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per firm data conventions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the write-off amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency matters and cross-border client billing. Enables currency-normalized financial reporting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the write-off amount to the firm\'s functional (home) currency at the time of posting. Relevant for multi-currency matters. Null for write-offs already denominated in the functional currency.']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'The type of fee arrangement under which the matter was being billed at the time of write-off. Contextualizes the write-off within the billing structure (e.g., AFA budget overrun write-offs differ analytically from hourly rate cap write-offs). Supports AFA performance and profitability analysis.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The firm\'s fiscal year and month in which the write-off is recognized for financial reporting purposes, formatted as YYYY-MM. Derived from gl_posting_date but stored explicitly to support period-based aggregation and financial close reporting without date arithmetic.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The write-off amount converted to the firm\'s functional (home) currency using the exchange_rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting, partner compensation calculations (PPP/PEP), and firm-wide realization rate analysis.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the write-off is posted to the General Ledger. Determines the financial period (month/quarter/year) in which the write-off impacts the firm\'s P&L and balance sheet. May differ from write_off_date due to period-end cut-off rules.']
  hours_written_off decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of billable hours associated with the write-off, applicable when the write-off relates to time entries. Null for expense or disbursement write-offs. Used to calculate effective realization rates and to measure the volume of written-off effort.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this write-off record is a reversal of a previously posted write-off (True) or an original write-off entry (False). Reversals restore WIP or AR balances and must be tracked separately for accurate net write-off reporting and GL reconciliation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or commentary entered by billing staff, the approving partner, or finance team regarding the write-off. May include client negotiation context, matter budget discussions, or collections history relevant to bad debt write-offs.']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the firm office (geographic location) associated with the matter or billing timekeeper at the time of write-off. Supports office-level financial performance reporting and regional realization rate analysis.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross value of the WIP or AR balance before the write-off is applied, expressed in the billing currency. Represents the full amount at standard rates prior to any reduction. Used as the denominator in realization rate calculations.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the write-off (e.g., client billing guideline non-compliance, rate cap exceeded, duplicate entry, uncollectable debt, client relationship courtesy, matter budget overrun). Sourced from Elite 3E or Aderant Expert reason code tables. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_guideline|rate_cap|duplicate_entry|bad_debt|courtesy|budget_overrun|staffing_level|task_not_approved — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explanation provided by the billing timekeeper or approving partner describing the specific circumstances justifying the write-off. Supplements the structured reason_code with contextual detail for audit and partner review purposes.']
  requires_secondary_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the write-off amount exceeds the firm\'s threshold requiring secondary approval (e.g., managing partner or finance committee sign-off in addition to the billing partner). Supports tiered approval workflow governance and write-off policy compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this write-off record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation between systems, and audit traceability in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier or transaction reference of this write-off record in the originating source system (Elite 3E or Aderant Expert). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  standard_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'The timekeeper\'s standard hourly billing rate at the time the original WIP entry was recorded. Used to calculate the standard value of hours written off and to measure rate realization impact. Null for flat-fee or expense write-offs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this write-off record was last modified in the system of record or the lakehouse Silver layer. Tracks status changes, approval updates, and corrections. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per firm data conventions.']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'The UTBMS activity code associated with the time entry being written off. Provides granular activity-level classification for task-based billing analysis and client billing guideline compliance reporting.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'The UTBMS task code associated with the time or expense entry being written off. Enables task-level write-off analysis aligned with client billing guidelines and AFA arrangements. Supports LEDES electronic billing compliance reporting.']
  wip_entry_date date [note: 'The date on which the original WIP time or expense entry being written off was recorded. Used to calculate WIP aging at the time of write-off and to assess how long unbilled time was carried before being written off. Null for AR (post-billing) write-offs.']
  write_down_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The partial reduction amount when a write-down (as opposed to a full write-off) is applied. Represents the difference between original_amount and the revised billable amount. Null for full write-offs. Supports partial realization analysis.']
  write_off_category varchar [note: 'Distinguishes whether the write-off applies to professional fees (timekeeper charges), disbursements (client-incurred costs), firm expenses, accrued interest on overdue AR, or tax components. Drives GL account mapping and realization rate segmentation.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The business event date on which the write-off was formally approved and recorded. This is the principal real-world event date used for financial period close, WIP aging, and realization rate calculations. Distinct from the system audit timestamps.']
  write_off_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the write-off transaction, typically generated by Elite 3E or Aderant Expert. Used in partner approval workflows, GL postings, and audit trails. Format: WO-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  write_off_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the write-off transaction. Tracks the workflow from initial submission through partner approval, GL posting, and potential reversal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_approval|approved|rejected|reversed|posted|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  write_off_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the write-off by its business nature. WIP write-off reduces unbilled time/expense value; bad debt eliminates uncollectable AR; courtesy discount reflects client relationship adjustments; billing guideline reduction reflects client-mandated rate or task reductions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wip_write_off|bad_debt|courtesy_discount|billing_guideline_reduction|fee_waiver|expense_write_off — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of WIP or AR amounts formally written off or written down following partner approval. Captures write-off type (WIP write-off, bad debt write-off, courtesy discount, billing guideline reduction), original amount, write-off amount, write-off reason code, approving partner, approval date, and GL impact. Critical for realization rate calculations, profitability analysis, and financial period close.'
}

Table billing.retainer {
  retainer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the retainer arrangement record. Primary key for the retainer data product within the billing domain.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated Interest on Lawyers Trust Accounts (IOLTA) or client trust account record in the trust accounting domain. Governs the custodial account from which retainer drawdowns are funded.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Retainers are contractual arrangements requiring documented authority. Retainer drawdowns require contractual verification, trust accounting audits trace to source agreements, disputes require governi']
  aml_kyc_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_kyc_program. Business justification: Retainers require AML/KYC verification before client funds are accepted (SRA Accounts Rules, FATF). Linking each retainer to the AML/KYC program under which verification was performed is a named regul']
  doc_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.doc_execution. Business justification: Retainer arrangements are formalized by executed engagement letters or fee agreements. Linking retainer to the doc_execution record proves the retainer was formally executed — critical for trust accou']
  fee_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.fee_arrangement. Business justification: Retainers are often governed by or linked to a specific fee arrangement that defines how retainer funds are drawn down, replenished, and applied to invoices. Currently retainer has fee_arrangement_typ']
  letter_of_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.letter_of_engagement. Business justification: Retainer agreements are established per the terms documented in the letter of engagement. Trust accounting and retainer drawdown rules must reference the governing LOE to ensure compliance with agreed']
  matter_opening_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.matter_opening_request. Business justification: Matter opening requests include a requires_trust_account flag that mandates retainer establishment. Tracing a retainer to the matter opening request that required it is a standard trust accounting com']
  matter_risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.matter_risk_profile. Business justification: AML/KYC risk reporting: retainer adequacy and kyc_verified_flag are assessed against the matter risk profile (aml_risk_flag, sanctions_screening_required_flag). Legal compliance teams link retainers t']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Retainers in legal firms are frequently scoped to a specific practice area (e.g., employment law retainer, IP prosecution retainer). Legal finance teams track retainer utilization and replenishment by']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client for whom this retainer arrangement has been established. Links the retainer to the client master record.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.request. Business justification: Intake requests specify whether a retainer is required as a condition of engagement. Linking retainers to the authorizing intake request supports trust account compliance audits and AML/KYC regulatory']
  retainer_ledes_client_matter_id varchar [note: 'The client-assigned matter identifier formatted per the Legal Electronic Data Exchange Standard (LEDES) specification. Required for electronic billing submissions to corporate clients and insurance carriers that mandate LEDES-format invoices.']
  retainer_matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific matter associated with this retainer. Null for general retainers that span all matters for a client; populated for matter-specific retainers.']
  sar_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sar_filing. Business justification: Retainer receipts are screened for AML red flags and may trigger SAR (large cash retainers, third-party funding, high-risk jurisdictions). Real business process: client funds acceptance and AML compli']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.tier. Business justification: Retainer terms (amount, replenishment thresholds, drawdown rules) are tier-dependent in legal firms. Tier 1 clients receive different retainer structures than standard clients. Legal finance teams str']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the retainer arrangement was formally approved by the responsible partner or governance authority. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the partner or authorised signatory who approved the establishment of this retainer arrangement. Required for governance and audit trail purposes.']
  auto_apply_to_invoice_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether retainer funds should be automatically applied to reduce the outstanding balance of invoices upon generation, without requiring manual partner approval for each drawdown.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed frequency at which fees are drawn against the retainer and invoices are generated. Drives the billing cycle schedule in the practice management system.']
  billing_office_code varchar [note: 'The office or cost centre code responsible for billing and administering this retainer. Used for revenue allocation, profit centre reporting, and Profit Per Partner (PPP) / Profit Per Equity Partner (PEP) analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this retainer record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the retainer amount, balance, and all drawdown transactions are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency billing for international clients.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current available balance of the retainer after all drawdowns and replenishments have been applied. Represents the remaining funds authorised for future billing drawdowns. Updated upon each drawdown event or replenishment receipt.']
  drawdown_authorization_rule varchar [note: 'The rule governing when and how funds may be drawn from the retainer balance against earned fees or disbursements. Automatic drawdown applies retainer funds upon invoice generation; manual approval requires explicit partner sign-off; threshold-triggered drawdown applies when WIP reaches a defined level; periodic scheduled drawdown occurs on a fixed calendar basis.']
  drawdown_via_payment varchar [note: 'FK to billing.payment.payment_id — Retainer drawdowns are processed as payment applications. This FK connects retainer balance management to the payment/AR cycle.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the retainer arrangement becomes binding and drawdowns may commence. Corresponds to the commencement date stated in the Letter of Engagement (LOE) or retainer agreement.']
  evergreen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this retainer is structured as an evergreen arrangement, meaning the client is contractually obligated to replenish the balance to the target amount whenever it falls below the replenishment threshold. Drives automated replenishment demand workflows.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the retainer arrangement expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended or evergreen retainers with no fixed end date. After this date, no further drawdowns are authorised without renewal.']
  interest_bearing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retainer funds held in the associated trust account are subject to interest accrual under IOLTA or client-specific interest arrangements. True if interest is earned and must be reported or remitted.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'The primary legal jurisdiction governing the retainer arrangement and the associated client engagement. Determines applicable professional conduct rules, trust accounting regulations, and tax treatment. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country codes.']
  kyc_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client associated with this retainer has completed Know Your Client (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) verification checks prior to the retainer being activated. Mandatory for regulatory compliance before funds are accepted.']
  last_drawdown_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent drawdown was applied against this retainer. Used for activity monitoring, dormancy detection, and collections follow-up.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this retainer record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronisation, and audit trail purposes across the billing domain.']
  last_replenishment_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent replenishment payment was received and applied to this retainer balance. Used for cash flow monitoring and client payment behaviour analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing supplementary information about the retainer arrangement, including special billing instructions, client-specific conditions, or internal administrative notes not captured in structured fields.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial monetary amount deposited or committed by the client when the retainer arrangement was established. Represents the starting balance before any drawdowns or replenishments. Expressed in the billing currency.']
  practice_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the practice group (e.g., Corporate, Litigation, Intellectual Property, Employment) under which this retainer is administered. Supports revenue reporting by practice area and Revenue Per Equity Partner (RPE) analytics.']
  refundable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any unused retainer balance is refundable to the client upon matter closure or retainer termination. Governs the treatment of residual balances in the billing close-out process.']
  replenishment_demand_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent replenishment demand was issued to the client. Used to track outstanding replenishment requests and calculate days outstanding for collections follow-up.']
  replenishment_due_date date [note: 'The contractual due date by which the client must remit the replenishment amount following a demand. Drives collections escalation workflows when payment is not received by this date.']
  replenishment_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount to which the retainer balance should be restored upon replenishment. May equal the original_amount or a separately negotiated top-up level. Used to calculate the replenishment demand sent to the client.']
  replenishment_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum balance level at which a replenishment request is automatically triggered and sent to the client. When current_balance falls at or below this threshold, the firm initiates a top-up demand. Critical for evergreen retainer management.']
  retainer_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the retainer arrangement. Used in client correspondence, billing statements, and engagement letters. Format: RET-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  retainer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the retainer arrangement. Active indicates funds are available for drawdown; exhausted indicates the balance has been fully applied; replenishment_pending indicates a top-up request has been issued to the client; closed indicates the arrangement has been formally terminated.']
  retainer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the retainer arrangement by its commercial structure. General retainers cover all matters for a client; matter-specific retainers are scoped to a single matter; evergreen retainers auto-replenish; security retainers are held as collateral; advance payment retainers are pre-payments for future services.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fees drawn against this retainer are exempt from applicable taxes (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax) based on the client\'s tax status or the nature of the legal services provided.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the retainer arrangement was formally closed or terminated, either upon matter completion, client withdrawal, or firm-initiated closure. Null for active retainers.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the retainer arrangement was closed or terminated. Used for client relationship analytics and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  total_drawn_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary amount drawn down from this retainer since its establishment. Represents the sum of all drawdown transactions applied against earned fees and disbursements. Used for retainer utilisation reporting and client billing transparency.']
  total_replenished_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary amount received from the client as retainer replenishments since the arrangement was established. Tracks all top-up receipts to support reconciliation and client account management.']
  under_fee_arrangement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.fee_arrangement.fee_arrangement_id — Retainers are a component of the overall fee arrangement with a client. Linking retainer to fee_arrangement ensures the retainer terms are governed by the broader commercial agreement.']
  wip_drawdown_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum Work in Progress (WIP) amount that may accumulate before a mandatory drawdown against the retainer is triggered. Prevents unbilled WIP from exceeding the retainer balance and ensures timely billing.']

  Note: 'Master record of client retainer arrangements including general retainers, matter-specific retainers, and evergreen retainers. Tracks retainer amount, replenishment threshold, current balance, drawdown history, retainer type, and associated trust account reference. Distinct from IOLTA trust accounts (owned by the trust domain) — this entity governs the commercial retainer agreement terms and drawdown authorization rules within the billing cycle.'
}

Table billing.guideline {
  guideline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each billing guideline record. Primary key for the billing_guideline data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Billing guidelines are contractual obligations embedded in or referenced by service agreements. Invoice review validates billing against contractual guidelines, disputes cite agreement clauses, guidel']
  doc_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.doc_template. Business justification: Billing guidelines mandate specific invoice templates, narrative formats, or LEDES formats. Essential for automated billing compliance, client-specific formatting requirements, and reducing billing re']
  matter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific matter to which this guideline applies. When populated, the guideline is matter-specific; when null, the guideline applies at the client level across all matters.']
  organisation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client for whom this billing guideline applies. Links the guideline to the client master record for enforcement during prebilling review and LEDES submission validation.']
  outside_counsel_guideline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.outside_counsel_guideline. Business justification: Billing guidelines in legal firms are directly derived from client-issued Outside Counsel Guidelines (OCGs). This link enables OCG compliance enforcement — rate caps, billing format mandates, expense ']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Billing guidelines are often practice-area-specific (e.g., IP guidelines differ from litigation). Practice area determines applicable guidelines, rate caps, and compliance rules used in invoice valida']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Billing guidelines in legal services are directly mandated by regulatory obligations (e.g., SRA Accounts Rules, price transparency rules). Compliance teams track which regulatory obligation each billi']
  superseded_by_guideline_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the newer billing_guideline record that replaces this one when guideline_status is superseded. Enables version chain traceability for audit and historical billing compliance review.']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.tier. Business justification: Billing guidelines are tier-specific in legal firms (different rate caps, expense limits, and timekeeper approval requirements apply to different client tiers). Legal billing compliance teams apply an']
  afa_type varchar [note: 'The fee arrangement type governed by this guideline. Identifies whether the guideline applies to hourly billing, a flat fee, contingency, blended rate, capped fee, or success fee arrangement. Aligns with the firm\'s AFA (Alternative Fee Arrangement) billing model.']
  applies_to_expenses boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing guideline applies to expense/disbursement entries in addition to or instead of time entries. When True, expense line items are subject to the same rule enforcement during prebilling review.']
  applies_to_time_entries boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing guideline applies to time entries. When True, time entry records are subject to the rule enforcement during prebilling review and LEDES submission validation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the partner, billing manager, or client representative who approved this billing guideline for enforcement. Provides accountability and audit trail for guideline activation.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this billing guideline was formally approved for enforcement. Distinct from effective_from, which is the date the rule becomes binding on billing entries.']
  block_billing_prohibited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether block billing (combining multiple tasks into a single time entry without itemisation) is prohibited under this guideline. When True, prebilling review must flag any time entries containing multiple tasks in a single narrative.']
  budget_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total matter-level or phase-level budget ceiling imposed by the client under this guideline. When WIP (Work in Progress) approaches or exceeds this cap, alerts are triggered for partner review before further billing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this billing guideline record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this billing guideline becomes binding and enforceable. Time entries and expenses incurred on or after this date are subject to the rule. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this billing guideline ceases to be enforceable. Null indicates an open-ended guideline with no defined expiry. Used to determine which version of a guideline applies to a given billing period.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'The system action triggered when a non-compliant entry is detected during prebilling review or LEDES submission validation. hard_block prevents invoice issuance; soft_warning alerts the billing team; write_off automatically reduces the entry; require_approval routes to partner; flag_for_review marks for manual review.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any agreed exceptions or carve-outs to this billing guideline, such as approved deviations negotiated with the client or specific matter circumstances where the rule does not apply.']
  expense_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable disbursement or expense amount per transaction or per category as defined by this guideline. Populated when rule_type is expense_limit. Used to flag non-compliant expense entries during prebilling review.']
  guideline_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the billing rule, including any conditions, exceptions, or qualifications. Provides the complete business context for the guideline as communicated by the client or as agreed in the engagement letter.']
  guideline_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or short title of the billing guideline, such as \'Block Billing Prohibition\' or \'Senior Associate Rate Cap\'. Used for display in prebilling dashboards and compliance reports.']
  guideline_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating document or instrument from which this billing guideline derives its authority. Values: ocg (client-issued Outside Counsel Guidelines), engagement_letter (Letter of Engagement / LOE), standing_instruction (ongoing client instruction), matter_specific_agreement (matter-level side agreement), court_order (court-mandated billing restriction).']
  guideline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billing guideline. Active guidelines are enforced during prebilling review. Superseded guidelines are retained for historical audit. Pending_approval guidelines are awaiting client or partner sign-off before enforcement.']
  invoice_submission_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days after the billing period end date within which the firm must submit an invoice to the client. Late submissions beyond this threshold may be subject to write-off or rejection per the OCG.']
  invoice_submission_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency at which invoices must be submitted to the client under this guideline. Governs billing cycle compliance and WIP (Work in Progress) management cadence.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The legal jurisdiction (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country code) to which this billing guideline applies. Relevant for multi-jurisdictional matters where different billing rules may apply per jurisdiction.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which this billing guideline was reviewed for continued applicability and accuracy. Supports periodic OCG review cycles and ensures guidelines remain current with client instructions.']
  ledes_billing_format varchar [note: 'The required LEDES electronic billing format version mandated by the client for invoice submission under this guideline. Governs the file format used when submitting invoices electronically to the client or their e-billing platform.']
  minimum_time_increment_mins varchar [note: 'The minimum billable time increment in minutes permitted under this guideline (e.g., 6 minutes = 0.1 hour, 15 minutes = 0.25 hour). Time entries rounded to increments larger than this value may be flagged as non-compliant.']
  narrative_min_length varchar [note: 'Minimum number of characters required in a time entry or expense narrative to satisfy the client\'s narrative requirement under this guideline. Entries with narratives shorter than this threshold are flagged during prebilling review.']
  new_timekeeper_rate_freeze boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has imposed a rate freeze on newly introduced timekeepers, requiring them to bill at the same rate as the timekeeper they replace or at a pre-agreed rate. Common OCG provision to control staffing cost escalation.']
  ocg_section_reference varchar [note: 'Specific section, clause, or paragraph number within the OCG document that contains the rule captured by this guideline record. Supports precise audit trail and dispute resolution when a billing entry is challenged.']
  rate_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly billing rate permitted under this guideline for the applicable timekeeper level or role. Populated only when rule_type is rate_cap. Enforced during prebilling review to flag time entries exceeding the agreed rate ceiling.']
  rate_cap_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the rate_cap_amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required when rate_cap_amount is populated to ensure multi-currency billing compliance.']
  rate_increase_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days advance notice the firm must provide to the client before implementing a rate increase under this guideline. Enforces the client\'s OCG requirement for rate change notification.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the client or law firm to uniquely reference this Outside Counsel Guideline (OCG) or billing instruction document. Used for cross-referencing with client-issued OCG documents.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the billing rule for reporting and filtering purposes. Segments guidelines into operational categories: timekeeper (rate and staffing rules), expense (disbursement rules), invoice_format (LEDES/UTBMS formatting), matter_budget (budget and cap rules), task_code (UTBMS task code restrictions), narrative (time entry description requirements).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the billing rule being enforced. Defines the nature of the compliance requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rate_cap|timekeeper_approval|task_code_restriction|narrative_requirement|block_billing_prohibition|expense_limit|staffing_restriction|budget_cap|write_off_threshold|invoice_format_requirement — promote to reference product]']
  timekeeper_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether timekeepers must be pre-approved by the client before their time can be billed under this guideline. When True, any time entry from a non-approved timekeeper is flagged as non-compliant during prebilling review.']
  timekeeper_level varchar [note: 'The seniority or role classification of the timekeeper to which this guideline applies. Used to scope rate caps and staffing restrictions to specific timekeeper grades. \'all\' indicates the rule applies universally regardless of timekeeper level.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this billing guideline record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  utbms_activity_code varchar [note: 'The UTBMS activity code (e.g., A101 Plan and Prepare, A102 Research) to which this guideline applies. Used in conjunction with utbms_task_code to restrict or govern specific task-activity combinations in LEDES invoice submissions.']
  utbms_task_code varchar [note: 'The UTBMS task code to which this guideline applies when rule_type is task_code_restriction. Identifies the specific legal task category (e.g., L110 Fact Investigation, L120 Analysis/Strategy) that is restricted or governed by this rule.']
  wip_aging_limit_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days that unbilled WIP (Work in Progress) may remain outstanding before it must be billed or written off per the client\'s guideline. Supports WIP management and collections compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record of client-specific billing guidelines and outside counsel guidelines (OCG) that govern invoice compliance requirements. Captures guideline source (client-issued OCG, engagement letter, standing instruction), rule type (rate cap, timekeeper approval, task code restriction, narrative requirement, block billing prohibition, expense limit), effective date, and enforcement action. Used during prebilling review and LEDES submission validation to flag non-compliant entries before invoice issuance.'
}

Table billing.invoice_dispute {
  invoice_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each invoice dispute record. Primary key for the invoice_dispute data product within the billing domain.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Invoice disputes citing billing guideline violations or contractual terms require direct access to governing agreement. Dispute resolution reviews contractual billing terms, escalation workflows pull ']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: Invoice disputes are raised by a specific client contact. The plain attributes `client_contact_email` and `client_contact_name` on invoice_dispute are denormalized representations of client.client_con']
  credit_note_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_note. Business justification: Invoice disputes often result in credit notes being issued to adjust the disputed amount. Currently invoice_dispute has credit_note_number (string) but no FK to the credit_note record. Adding credit_n']
  disputed_time_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific time entry record being disputed, where the dispute is at the line-item level. Null when the dispute applies to multiple entries or the full invoice. Enables granular WIP (Work in Progress) and realization analytics.']
  docket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to court.docket. Business justification: Invoice disputes arise from specific litigation matters/dockets. Essential for dispute resolution, client relationship management, and matter-level billing accuracy in court cases.']
  indemnity_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.indemnity_claim. Business justification: Invoice disputes can escalate to professional indemnity claims (alleged overbilling, negligence, guideline violations). Real business process: dispute escalation to PI claim requires linking disputes ']
  legal_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.legal_document. Business justification: Invoice disputes reference supporting documents (billing guidelines, engagement letters, court orders, fee agreements) for resolution. Critical for dispute substantiation, write-off justification, and']
  letter_of_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intake.letter_of_engagement. Business justification: Invoice disputes are adjudicated against LOE terms covering agreed scope, rates, and billing guidelines. Disputes teams must pull the LOE to determine whether a disputed charge was within agreed scope']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.obligation. Business justification: Invoice disputes in legal services arise from specific contract obligations (rate caps, expense limits, billing guideline clauses). Linking to the specific obligation being disputed enables obligation']
  pi_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.pi_claim. Business justification: Dispute-to-PI-claim escalation tracking: invoice disputes that allege negligence or breach of duty escalate to formal PI claims. Legal risk teams need to link the originating invoice_dispute to the re']
  practice_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.practice_area. Business justification: Invoice disputes are resolved by practice area leadership with area-specific resolution authority and client relationship management. Practice area determines dispute resolution approach, approval aut']
  primary_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent invoice against which this dispute has been raised. Links the dispute to the originating billing document in the accounts receivable cycle.']
